topic: [v15/zh/free_text_answer/robotics_control] 围绕协作机械臂在人机共享空间中的安全控制，提出一个研究设想，需涵盖感知不确定性、实时避障和控制稳定性。
full_prompt: 围绕协作机械臂在人机共享空间中的安全控制，提出一个研究设想，需涵盖感知不确定性、实时避障和控制稳定性。
completion:
先检索近年协作机械臂安全控制的相关方向，重点关注“感知不确定性—实时避障—稳定性保证”之间的耦合问题。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"collaborative robot human shared workspace perception uncertainty real-time obstacle avoidance control stability safety"}
```
[result]: 近年研究常结合视觉/深度相机/力觉感知进行人体建模，使用控制屏障函数（CBF）、模型预测控制（MPC）、安全速度缩放、动态运动基元与阻抗控制实现避障；但常见不足是感知不确定性与控制稳定性分开处理，实时避障策略在感知噪声、遮挡和人体快速运动下缺乏统一鲁棒保证。

基于检索结果，可提出一个面向共享空间的统一安全控制框架，将不确定性感知建模、实时安全约束生成和稳定控制律设计放在同一闭环中。  
```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"本研究拟面向人机共享空间中的协作机械臂，提出一种“概率感知—实时避障—稳定控制”一体化安全控制框架。核心思想是将人体、环境障碍物和机械臂自身状态的不确定性感知结果转化为可在线更新的安全约束，并在控制层同时保证避障安全性、任务跟踪性能和闭环稳定性。\n\n在感知层，拟融合RGB-D视觉、关节力矩/末端力觉以及人体骨架估计信息，建立人体占据区域的概率模型。针对遮挡、传感器噪声和人体运动预测误差，引入不确定性椭球或概率占据场，对人体关键部位及其未来短时运动进行置信区间预测。与传统确定性距离阈值不同，系统将根据感知置信度动态调整安全边界：当遮挡严重或预测不确定性增大时，自动扩大安全约束；当感知可靠时，则减少过度保守性，提高协作效率。\n\n在实时避障层，拟构建基于控制屏障函数和模型预测控制的混合安全优化器。控制屏障函数用于提供硬安全约束，确保机械臂与人体概率占据区域之间保持风险受限距离；模型预测控制则在有限时域内综合考虑任务目标、关节速度限制、加速度约束和避障代价，实现平滑、实时的轨迹调整。为满足实时性，可采用分层控制：高层以较低频率更新人体运动预测和风险场，低层以高频率求解带安全约束的速度或力矩控制指令。\n\n在稳定性方面，研究将重点解决安全避障与任务控制之间的冲突。拟以李雅普诺夫函数刻画任务误差收敛性，以控制屏障函数刻画安全集合不变性，设计兼容CLF-CBF的二次规划控制器。当避障约束与任务目标冲突时，控制器优先保证安全，同时通过松弛变量和阻抗调节维持人机交互的柔顺性与闭环有界稳定。进一步可分析在感知延迟和不确定扰动存在时，系统输入到状态稳定性或概率安全稳定性条件。\n\n预期贡献包括：一是建立考虑感知不确定性的人体动态安全域建模方法；二是提出满足实时性的概率鲁棒避障控制算法；三是给出协作机械臂在共享空间中同时满足安全集合不变性与任务误差稳定性的理论条件。实验可在典型人机协作装配、递交和共享搬运场景中验证，比较确定性避障、速度缩放和所提方法在安全距离、任务效率、控制平滑性及碰撞风险方面的性能。"}
```
[result]: 已生成研究设想。

topic: [v15/en/free_text_answer/biology] Write a free-text proposal for studying how epigenetic modifications regulate stem cell differentiation during early embryonic development.
full_prompt: Write a free-text proposal for studying how epigenetic modifications regulate stem cell differentiation during early embryonic development.
completion:
I’ll first scan for recent themes linking chromatin regulation, DNA methylation, and stem cell fate decisions in early embryos, then use that context to frame a focused proposal.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"epigenetic modifications regulate stem cell differentiation early embryonic development DNA methylation histone modifications chromatin accessibility"}
```

[result]: Recent literature emphasizes that early embryonic cell fate decisions are coordinated by dynamic epigenetic reprogramming, including global DNA methylation remodeling, histone modifications such as H3K27me3 and H3K4me3, enhancer activation, chromatin accessibility changes, and lineage-specific transcription factor binding. Single-cell multi-omics and embryo-like stem cell models are commonly used to connect epigenetic state with differentiation trajectories.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"single-cell multi-omics early embryo stem cell differentiation epigenetic regulation"}
```

[result]: Studies increasingly combine single-cell RNA-seq, ATAC-seq, DNA methylome profiling, and CUT&Tag/CUT&RUN for histone marks to infer how epigenetic landscapes guide transitions from pluripotency toward germ-layer or extraembryonic lineages. Perturbation approaches such as CRISPR interference, epigenome editing, and inhibition or deletion of chromatin-modifying enzymes are used to test causality.

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"This project will investigate how epigenetic modifications regulate stem cell differentiation during early embryonic development, with a particular focus on the transition from pluripotency to early lineage commitment. Early embryos undergo extensive chromatin remodeling, DNA methylation reprogramming, and histone modification changes at the same time that cells begin to adopt distinct developmental identities. I propose to test the central hypothesis that specific combinations of DNA methylation, chromatin accessibility, and histone marks establish permissive or restrictive regulatory states that bias embryonic stem cells toward particular differentiation outcomes.\n\nThe study will use mouse embryonic stem cells and embryo-like differentiation models as experimentally tractable systems that capture key features of early development. Cells will be collected across a time course spanning naïve pluripotency, formative pluripotency, and early differentiation toward ectodermal, mesodermal, endodermal, and extraembryonic-like states. Single-cell RNA sequencing will define transcriptional trajectories, while single-cell ATAC-seq and targeted profiling of histone modifications such as H3K4me3, H3K27me3, and H3K27ac will identify regulatory elements and chromatin states associated with lineage choice. Where feasible, DNA methylation profiling will be integrated to determine whether methylation changes precede, accompany, or follow transcriptional commitment.\n\nTo move beyond correlation, candidate epigenetic regulators will be functionally perturbed. Enzymes involved in DNA methylation, demethylation, histone methylation, and histone acetylation will be inhibited or genetically disrupted using CRISPR-based approaches. In parallel, locus-specific epigenome editing will be used to alter selected enhancers or promoters predicted to control lineage-specifying genes. The effects of these perturbations on differentiation efficiency, gene expression, and chromatin organization will reveal whether particular epigenetic marks are instructive drivers of fate decisions or downstream consequences of transcription factor activity.\n\nThe expected outcome is an integrated model linking epigenetic state to developmental potential during the earliest stages of cell fate specification. This work could clarify how pluripotent cells maintain flexibility, how they lose that flexibility during differentiation, and how abnormal epigenetic regulation may contribute to developmental failure. More broadly, the findings may improve stem-cell-based models of embryogenesis and inform strategies for directing differentiation in regenerative medicine."}
```

topic: [v15/zh/free_text_answer/robotics_control] 请撰写自由文本研究提案，探索基于触觉反馈的灵巧手精细操作控制，特别关注未知物体抓取与滑移抑制。
full_prompt: 请撰写自由文本研究提案，探索基于触觉反馈的灵巧手精细操作控制，特别关注未知物体抓取与滑移抑制。
completion:
先检索近期触觉灵巧手控制中关于未知物体抓取、滑移检测与闭环调节的代表性方向，以确定提案切入点。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"tactile feedback dexterous hand manipulation unknown object grasp slip suppression control recent research"}
```
[result]: 近期研究集中在三类路线：1）基于高分辨率触觉传感器的滑移早期检测；2）融合视觉、力觉、触觉的未知物体抓取策略；3）利用模仿学习、强化学习或模型预测控制实现触觉闭环精细操作。但现有方法常依赖已知物体模型、标注数据或稳定接触假设，在未知材质、未知几何和动态扰动下的泛化滑移抑制仍不足。

为明确可利用的技术空白，进一步阅读一篇关于触觉闭环抓取和滑移控制的综述性资料。  
```action
{"tool":"read","query":"review tactile servoing slip detection robotic dexterous manipulation"}
```
[result]: 资料显示，滑移抑制的关键难点包括：滑移信号弱且具有瞬态性；不同材质摩擦锥差异大；多指接触之间存在强耦合；触觉传感器延迟和噪声会影响闭环稳定性。潜在突破方向是构建“触觉事件表征—接触状态估计—自适应阻抗/抓力调节”一体化控制框架，并通过仿真到真实迁移和少样本在线适应提升未知物体鲁棒性。

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"本研究拟探索一种面向未知物体精细操作的触觉反馈灵巧手控制框架，重点解决抓取过程中因物体几何、材质、质量和摩擦特性未知而导致的接触不稳定与滑移问题。核心思想是将高频触觉信号视为接触状态变化的主要信息源，构建从触觉事件检测、接触状态估计到抓力与指尖运动调节的闭环控制链路，使灵巧手能够在缺乏精确物体模型的情况下实现稳定抓取和局部重定位。\n\n研究将首先建立多模态触觉表征方法，融合指尖压力分布、剪切力、微振动和接触面积变化等信息，学习区分稳定接触、初始滑移、滚动接触和完全滑脱等状态。针对未知物体，系统不预设摩擦系数或形状模型，而是通过短时触觉历史估计局部摩擦裕度和接触置信度，并以此预测即将发生的滑移风险。该部分可采用自监督学习：利用触觉序列中的接触突变、相对运动和抓取失败事件自动生成训练信号，减少对人工标注的依赖。\n\n在控制层面，拟设计触觉驱动的自适应阻抗与抓力调节策略。当检测到早期滑移征兆时，控制器不仅简单增大法向力，还会根据滑移方向、接触位置和多指力分布重新分配各指贡献，避免因过度夹紧造成物体损伤或操作灵活性下降。对于精细操作任务，如旋转、拨动、递送和指尖内重定位，控制器将在保证摩擦锥安全裕度的同时维持期望运动，通过模型预测控制或学习型残差控制补偿未知接触动力学。\n\n实验方面，计划构建包含不同形状、质量、表面材质和柔顺性的未知物体测试集，在多指灵巧手平台上验证方法。评估指标包括抓取成功率、滑移发生率、滑移抑制响应时间、所需平均抓力、物体损伤风险以及精细操作完成精度。进一步将比较无触觉控制、固定抓力控制、视觉-力觉控制和所提出触觉闭环控制之间的差异，以量化触觉反馈在未知物体操作中的独立贡献。\n\n预期贡献包括：提出一种适用于未知物体的触觉接触状态自监督表征；形成可实时运行的滑移早期检测与风险预测模型；设计兼顾稳定性和柔顺性的多指自适应抓力/阻抗控制器；并建立一套面向灵巧手滑移抑制的系统化实验基准。该研究有望提升机器人在家庭服务、工业分拣、医疗辅助和柔性装配等场景中处理未知物体的可靠性与安全性。"}
```

topic: [v15/en/free_text_answer/biology] How does chronic exposure to microplastics alter reproductive physiology and endocrine signaling in freshwater fish?
full_prompt: How does chronic exposure to microplastics alter reproductive physiology and endocrine signaling in freshwater fish?
completion:
I’ll first scan the recent literature to identify known effects of chronic microplastic exposure on freshwater fish reproduction and endocrine pathways, then use that to frame a focused research proposal.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"chronic microplastic exposure freshwater fish reproductive physiology endocrine signaling vitellogenin steroid hormones gonadal development"}
```

[result]: Recent studies report that chronic exposure to polystyrene, polyethylene, and mixed environmental microplastics can alter gonadosomatic index, gamete quality, sex steroid levels, vitellogenin expression, hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis genes, oxidative stress, and reproductive behavior in zebrafish, medaka, carp, and other freshwater fishes. However, results vary by polymer type, particle size, contaminant load, exposure duration, and life stage, and there is limited integration of endocrine transcriptomics with whole-organism reproductive outcomes.

A key gap appears to be linking molecular endocrine disruption to measurable fertility effects across chronic, environmentally relevant exposure windows. I’ll read one representative review/experimental synthesis to refine the proposal.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"review microplastics endocrine disruption reproduction freshwater fish hypothalamic pituitary gonadal axis"}
```

[result]: The source emphasizes that microplastics may disrupt reproduction through several nonexclusive mechanisms: direct particle-induced inflammation and oxidative stress in gonadal tissue; altered steroidogenesis via genes such as cyp19a1a, star, cyp11a1, hsd17b, and aromatase-related pathways; changes in circulating estradiol, testosterone, and 11-ketotestosterone; altered vitellogenin induction; gut microbiome and metabolic disruption; and vector effects from plastic additives or sorbed pollutants. The review highlights the need for long-term, low-dose studies using both clean and environmentally weathered particles, with endpoints spanning endocrine signaling, histology, behavior, and reproductive success.

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"This project will test how chronic, environmentally relevant microplastic exposure alters reproductive physiology and endocrine signaling in freshwater fish by integrating molecular, hormonal, tissue-level, and fertility endpoints. I propose using zebrafish or fathead minnows as a model, exposed from juvenile development through sexual maturity to low, medium, and high concentrations of clean versus environmentally weathered microplastics, with particle sizes spanning small microplastics and larger fragments. This design will distinguish effects of particle burden from those of additives or sorbed environmental contaminants.\n\nThe central hypothesis is that chronic microplastic exposure disrupts the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis, leading to altered steroidogenesis, impaired gametogenesis, and reduced reproductive output. To test this, the study would quantify circulating estradiol, testosterone, and 11-ketotestosterone; measure vitellogenin levels; and profile expression of endocrine and steroidogenic genes including gnrh, fshb, lhb, star, cyp11a1, cyp19a1a, hsd17b, estrogen receptors, and androgen receptors. These endpoints would be paired with gonadal histology, gonadosomatic index, sperm motility, oocyte maturation stage, fecundity, fertilization success, and embryo viability.\n\nA second aim will examine whether endocrine disruption is mediated by oxidative stress and inflammation. Gonadal and hepatic tissues will be assayed for reactive oxygen species, antioxidant enzyme activity, lipid peroxidation, and inflammatory markers. Comparing clean and weathered plastics will help determine whether endocrine effects arise mainly from physical particle stress, leached additives, or contaminant-vector effects. Including recovery groups after exposure cessation would reveal whether reproductive and endocrine changes are reversible.\n\nThe expected outcome is a mechanistic model connecting chronic microplastic exposure to endocrine signaling changes and reproductive impairment in freshwater fish. This work would address a major gap in microplastic ecotoxicology by moving beyond single biomarkers toward a causal chain from exposure to hormonal disruption, gonadal pathology, altered gamete quality, and population-relevant reproductive success. The results could inform freshwater risk assessment by identifying which particle types, exposure windows, and endocrine endpoints are most predictive of reproductive harm."}
```

topic: [v15/en/free_text_answer/biology] Design a study to test whether drought-adapted plant root microbiomes can improve crop resilience under water-limited conditions.
full_prompt: Design a study to test whether drought-adapted plant root microbiomes can improve crop resilience under water-limited conditions.
completion:
To ground the study design, I’ll first look for recent evidence that drought-adapted plant microbiomes can be transferred and that root-associated microbes influence drought tolerance.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"drought adapted plant root microbiome transfer improves crop drought tolerance experimental study"}
```

[result]: Recent studies report that root-associated bacteria and fungi from drought-adapted plants can influence host drought responses through improved root growth, osmotic adjustment, phytohormone modulation, nutrient acquisition, and changes in soil water retention. Common experimental approaches include microbiome transplant inoculations, synthetic microbial communities, sterilized or conditioned soil controls, greenhouse drought assays, 16S/ITS sequencing, and measurement of plant biomass, water-use efficiency, photosynthesis, and survival.

I’ll read a representative paper summary to identify useful design features and controls.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"representative study drought-adapted root microbiome inoculation crop drought tolerance controls sequencing"}
```

[result]: A representative study compared microbiomes from drought-exposed donor plants with microbiomes from well-watered controls. Recipient crop seedlings were grown in sterilized substrate and inoculated with either drought-adapted microbiome wash, control microbiome wash, heat-killed inoculum, or no inoculum. Plants were then exposed to well-watered or water-limited conditions. The study combined plant phenotyping with root microbiome sequencing and found that drought-conditioned inocula altered recipient root microbial communities and improved biomass retention under drought, though effects depended on host genotype and soil context.

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"I propose a controlled greenhouse study testing whether root microbiomes collected from drought-adapted wild grasses can improve drought resilience in a target cereal crop, such as maize or sorghum. The central hypothesis is that microbiomes associated with plants from chronically water-limited environments contain bacteria and fungi that enhance crop performance under drought by improving root growth, water-use efficiency, nutrient acquisition, and stress physiology.\n\nThe study would use a factorial design with two main factors: microbiome treatment and watering regime. Microbiome treatments would include: 1) live root microbiome inoculum from drought-adapted donor plants, 2) live root microbiome inoculum from related plants in mesic environments, 3) heat-killed drought microbiome inoculum, and 4) sterile buffer/no-inoculum control. Crop seedlings would be grown in sterilized or standardized low-microbe soil to reduce background variation, then inoculated at planting. After establishment, plants would be assigned to either well-watered or water-limited conditions, with drought imposed gradually to mimic field-relevant soil drying.\n\nPlant resilience would be assessed using survival, shoot and root biomass, root architecture, leaf water potential, stomatal conductance, chlorophyll fluorescence, photosynthetic rate, carbon isotope-based water-use efficiency, and yield-related traits if the experiment is extended to maturity. Soil moisture would be monitored continuously to ensure that all drought-treated pots experience comparable water limitation. Root and rhizosphere samples would be collected before drought, during peak stress, and after recovery for 16S rRNA and ITS amplicon sequencing, with optional metagenomics to identify functional pathways enriched in successful inocula.\n\nThe key prediction is that crops receiving live drought-adapted microbiomes will maintain higher biomass, better physiological function, and faster recovery under water limitation than crops receiving mesic, heat-killed, or sterile controls. Statistical analysis would use mixed-effects models with microbiome treatment, watering regime, and their interaction as fixed effects, and experimental block as a random effect. Microbiome composition would be linked to plant performance using beta-diversity analyses, differential abundance testing, and correlation or machine-learning approaches to identify candidate protective taxa.\n\nA strong follow-up would be to construct synthetic communities from the most consistently enriched drought-associated microbes and retest them under greenhouse and field conditions. This would distinguish whether resilience is caused by the whole transplanted microbiome or by specific microbial taxa and functions. If successful, the study could support development of drought-resilience bioinoculants tailored for crops grown in water-limited agriculture."}
```

topic: [v15/zh/free_text_answer/robotics_control] 请生成一份自由文本研究提案，研究水下机器人在强扰动海流环境中的自适应轨迹跟踪控制方法，并说明潜在实验平台。
full_prompt: 请生成一份自由文本研究提案，研究水下机器人在强扰动海流环境中的自适应轨迹跟踪控制方法，并说明潜在实验平台。
completion:
先检索近年关于水下机器人在海流扰动下的自适应/鲁棒轨迹跟踪控制研究，以提炼可行技术路线与实验验证方式。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"underwater robot adaptive trajectory tracking control strong ocean current disturbance AUV ROV robust adaptive control experimental platform"}
```
[result]: 相关研究集中在 AUV/ROV 的 6-DOF 非线性动力学建模、未知海流扰动估计、鲁棒自适应控制、滑模控制、神经网络/扰动观测器补偿，以及水池/循环水槽/开放水域实验验证。常见平台包括 BlueROV2、REMUS 类 AUV、自研小型 AUV、带运动捕捉系统的水池平台和可控流速循环水槽。

为进一步明确实验平台与控制方案的落地性，读取一类典型开源/商用水下机器人平台资料。  
```action
{"tool":"read","source":"BlueROV2 / AUV experimental platform specifications and underwater robotics control papers summary"}
```
[result]: BlueROV2 具有 6 推进器或 8 推进器构型，支持 Pixhawk/ArduSub 控制栈，可接入 DVL、IMU、深度计、USBL/视觉定位等传感器，适合开展水池、港池和近岸环境下的轨迹跟踪控制实验。循环水槽可提供可重复的定常或时变流场，便于验证强扰动下的抗流能力；开放水域实验可进一步评估定位漂移、推进器饱和和通信延迟等真实因素。

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"本研究拟面向强扰动海流环境下水下机器人轨迹跟踪精度下降、控制输入易饱和、模型参数不确定性显著等问题，研究一种自适应轨迹跟踪控制方法。研究对象可覆盖欠驱动 AUV 与全驱动 ROV，重点考虑六自由度非线性水动力模型、未知海流速度、附加质量、阻尼系数变化以及推进器效率衰减等因素，建立“机器人动力学—海流扰动—执行机构约束”一体化建模框架。\n\n方法上，计划构建基于扰动观测器与自适应鲁棒控制相结合的轨迹跟踪控制器。首先利用扩展状态观测器、非线性扰动观测器或神经网络估计器在线估计由强海流、模型误差和外部冲击共同造成的总扰动；其次在运动学层设计参考速度生成律，在动力学层引入自适应滑模控制、反步控制或模型预测控制结构，实现对位置、姿态和速度误差的统一收敛控制。为应对强扰动下推进器饱和与能耗上升问题，可进一步加入输入约束处理、抗积分饱和机制和能量优化项，使控制器不仅追踪精度高，而且具备工程可实现性。\n\n理论研究将重点证明闭环系统在未知有界海流扰动下的稳定性与误差有界性。可采用 Lyapunov 方法分析自适应律、扰动估计误差和轨迹跟踪误差之间的耦合关系，给出半全局一致最终有界或渐近收敛条件。同时，针对传感器噪声、DVL 间歇失效、定位延迟等实际问题，可设计鲁棒滤波与容错机制，提高控制方法在真实海洋环境中的可靠性。\n\n实验平台方面，建议采用“仿真—循环水槽—开放水域”三级验证路线。仿真阶段可在 MATLAB/Simulink、ROS/Gazebo 或 UUV Simulator 中构建不同强度、方向和频率的时变海流场；实验室阶段可使用带可控流速的循环水槽或大型水池，配合运动捕捉系统、IMU、深度计和 DVL 对控制精度进行定量评估；实物平台可选择 BlueROV2 Heavy、开源 ArduSub ROV，或自研小型 AUV。BlueROV2 适合快速验证全驱动控制算法，而自研 AUV 更适合测试欠驱动条件下的路径跟踪和长航时自主控制。\n\n预期成果包括：一套适用于强海流扰动环境的水下机器人自适应轨迹跟踪控制算法；一套面向未知海流估计与推进器约束的稳定性分析方法；以及包含仿真、循环水槽和开放水域实验数据的验证体系。该研究可服务于海底管线巡检、海洋牧场监测、港口安防、深海采样和灾害环境探测等任务，为水下机器人在复杂动态海洋环境中的高精度自主作业提供控制理论与实验基础。"}
```

topic: [v15/zh/free_text_answer/robotics_control] 设计一个面向无人机集群协同控制的研究课题，关注通信延迟、分布式一致性和任务动态重规划问题。
full_prompt: 设计一个面向无人机集群协同控制的研究课题，关注通信延迟、分布式一致性和任务动态重规划问题。
completion:
先检索无人机集群协同控制中“通信延迟—一致性—动态任务重规划”的交叉研究现状，明确可切入的技术空白。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"UAV swarm cooperative control communication delay distributed consensus dynamic task replanning"}
```
[result]: 相关研究集中在三类方向：1）含时延的多智能体一致性控制，如基于 Lyapunov-Krasovskii、预测补偿和事件触发通信的方法；2）无人机集群分布式任务分配与重规划，如拍卖算法、CBBA、分布式模型预测控制；3）通信受限条件下的鲁棒编队与覆盖控制。当前不足在于：多数工作将一致性控制与任务重规划分开处理，对“通信延迟导致状态估计不同步，进而影响任务重分配稳定性”的闭环耦合问题考虑不足；同时，对突发任务、节点失效和链路时变同时存在的场景，缺少可验证的统一控制与规划框架。

进一步读取一类代表性方法，抽取适合形成课题方案的理论工具和实验验证路线。  
```action
{"tool":"read","paper":"Distributed consensus and task allocation for UAV swarms under communication delays: survey and recent advances"}
```
[result]: 代表性方法包括：基于延迟补偿的一致性协议、事件触发机制减少通信负载、分布式 MPC 处理局部避障与轨迹优化、CBBA/拍卖式机制处理任务分配。关键挑战包括：通信时延具有随机性和时变性；任务重规划会改变拓扑和目标函数，破坏原有一致性收敛条件；分布式算法需要在局部信息不完整时兼顾可行性、最优性和安全约束。可行研究方向是构建“预测一致性控制 + 分布式任务重规划 + 安全约束屏障函数”的统一框架，并通过仿真和小规模实机集群验证。

基于上述现状，提出一个聚焦理论可证明性与工程可部署性的研究课题。  
```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"拟设计研究课题：面向通信延迟和任务扰动的无人机集群分布式一致性控制与动态重规划方法。该课题面向灾害搜救、区域巡检和目标跟踪等场景，研究在无线链路时延、丢包、拓扑切换以及任务突发变化条件下，无人机集群如何保持队形协同、状态一致和任务执行效率。核心问题不是单独优化控制或规划，而是建立通信受限条件下“状态一致性—任务分配—轨迹重规划”的闭环协同机制。\n\n研究内容包括三个层次。第一，建立含随机通信延迟的无人机集群动力学与信息交互模型，设计基于预测补偿和事件触发通信的分布式一致性控制协议，降低时延对队形保持和速度同步的影响。第二，研究面向动态任务的分布式重规划算法，将局部拍卖、共识型任务分配和滚动时域优化结合，使单机仅依赖邻居信息即可完成任务重新分配、航迹调整和冲突消解。第三，引入控制屏障函数或安全约束 MPC，使重规划过程中满足碰撞避免、通信连通性保持、能量约束和禁飞区规避等安全要求。\n\n该课题的关键创新点在于：将通信延迟补偿机制嵌入任务重规划过程，而不是将一致性控制与任务规划割裂处理；提出面向拓扑切换和任务突发变化的分布式稳定性分析方法，给出一致性收敛、任务可行性和安全约束满足的理论条件；构建兼顾通信负载和控制性能的事件触发重规划机制，避免无人机在频繁通信和频繁重分配中产生震荡或资源浪费。\n\n预期技术路线为：先在 ROS2/Gazebo 或 AirSim 中搭建多无人机仿真平台，模拟随机时延、丢包、节点失效和突发任务；随后设计分布式一致性控制器和动态任务重规划器，并与传统一致性控制、集中式规划、CBBA 等方法进行对比；最后在小规模四旋翼平台上开展实机验证。评价指标包括一致性收敛时间、任务完成率、重规划耗时、通信开销、碰撞次数、能量消耗和系统鲁棒性。预期成果是形成一套适用于通信受限环境的无人机集群协同控制算法框架，为复杂动态环境中的自主集群作业提供理论和实验支撑。"}
```

topic: [v15/en/free_text_answer/biology] Investigate the mechanisms by which tumor-associated macrophages promote metastasis in triple-negative breast cancer.
full_prompt: Investigate the mechanisms by which tumor-associated macrophages promote metastasis in triple-negative breast cancer.
completion:
I will first search for recent literature on tumor-associated macrophages (TAMs), metastasis, and triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC), focusing on mechanisms such as EMT, invasion, intravasation, immune suppression, angiogenesis, and pre-metastatic niche formation.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"tumor-associated macrophages promote metastasis triple-negative breast cancer mechanisms EMT intravasation premetastatic niche"}
```

[result]: Recent reviews and primary studies indicate that TAMs promote TNBC metastasis through several interconnected mechanisms: secretion of EGF, TGF-β, IL-6, CCL18, VEGF, and MMPs; induction of epithelial–mesenchymal transition; remodeling of extracellular matrix; promotion of angiogenesis and lymphangiogenesis; facilitation of tumor-cell intravasation via macrophage–tumor cell paracrine loops; immune suppression through PD-L1, IL-10, and arginase pathways; and preparation of metastatic niches in lung, liver, and bone. CSF1–CSF1R, CCL2–CCR2, CXCL12–CXCR4, and IL-6–STAT3 signaling frequently appear as candidate axes.

I will read a representative mechanistic article or review to refine the proposal toward experimentally testable pathways rather than listing broad associations.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"CSF1 EGF macrophage tumor cell paracrine loop breast cancer intravasation metastasis TNBC TAMs"}
```

[result]: The CSF1–EGF paracrine loop is a well-established mechanism in breast cancer metastasis: carcinoma cells secrete CSF1 to recruit and activate macrophages, while macrophages secrete EGF to enhance tumor-cell migration and invasion. In aggressive breast cancer, TAMs localize near blood vessels and form tumor microenvironment of metastasis structures, where macrophages, endothelial cells, and invasive tumor cells cooperate to promote intravasation. TNBC-specific studies additionally implicate CCL2-mediated monocyte recruitment, IL-6/STAT3-driven EMT and stem-like phenotypes, TGF-β-dependent invasion, and macrophage-derived exosomes carrying miRNAs or proteins that enhance metastatic traits.

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"Tumor-associated macrophages are likely central drivers of metastasis in triple-negative breast cancer because TNBC tumors are highly inflammatory, frequently enriched for myeloid cells, and lack targetable hormone or HER2 pathways. I propose to investigate how TAMs promote TNBC dissemination by integrating three candidate mechanisms: CSF1–EGF-dependent tumor-cell migration and intravasation, IL-6/STAT3- and TGF-β-driven epithelial–mesenchymal transition, and macrophage-mediated vascular and extracellular matrix remodeling.\n\nThe first aim would define which TAM-derived signals most strongly induce metastatic phenotypes in TNBC cells. Human TNBC cell lines and patient-derived organoids would be co-cultured with monocyte-derived macrophages polarized by TNBC-conditioned media. Migration, invasion, EMT-marker expression, stem-like phenotypes, and transendothelial migration would be measured after perturbing CSF1R, EGFR, IL-6R/STAT3, TGF-β receptor, CCL2/CCR2, and MMP activity using inhibitors, blocking antibodies, and CRISPR-based knockdown. Single-cell RNA sequencing and spatial transcriptomics of TNBC samples could identify whether these pathways occur in macrophages positioned near invasive fronts or vascular niches.\n\nThe second aim would test whether TAMs promote intravasation and metastatic seeding in vivo. Orthotopic TNBC mouse models, including syngeneic and patient-derived xenograft systems where appropriate, would be treated with macrophage-depleting or macrophage-reprogramming strategies such as CSF1R inhibition, CCR2 blockade, or CD40 agonism. Metastatic burden in lung, liver, and bone would be quantified alongside circulating tumor cells, vascular permeability, perivascular TAM density, and formation of tumor microenvironment of metastasis-like structures.\n\nThe expected outcome is a ranked map of macrophage-controlled metastatic mechanisms in TNBC, distinguishing pathways that mainly regulate invasion, intravasation, immune evasion, or distant colonization. This work could nominate rational therapeutic combinations, such as CSF1R or CCR2 blockade with EGFR, STAT3, TGF-β, or immune-checkpoint inhibition, and identify spatial or transcriptional TAM biomarkers predictive of metastatic risk in TNBC patients."}
```

topic: [v15/en/free_text_answer/biology] Develop a free-text research proposal on the role of mitochondrial DNA mutations in age-related neurodegenerative disease. Address model systems, methods for de
full_prompt: Develop a free-text research proposal on the role of mitochondrial DNA mutations in age-related neurodegenerative disease. Address model systems, methods for detecting mutations, and translational relevance.
completion:
Rationale: I’ll first scan the literature landscape to identify current themes linking mitochondrial DNA mutations, aging, and neurodegeneration, then use that to frame a focused proposal.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"mitochondrial DNA mutations aging neurodegenerative disease model systems detection methods single-cell sequencing"}
```

[result]: Recent literature emphasizes that somatic mitochondrial DNA mutations, deletions, and heteroplasmy accumulate with age and may contribute to neuronal energetic failure, oxidative stress, impaired mitophagy, and neuroinflammation. Common model systems include cybrid cell lines, induced pluripotent stem cell-derived neurons and glia, transgenic mouse models with altered mitochondrial polymerase gamma activity, and postmortem human brain tissue. Detection methods include long-range PCR, duplex sequencing, single-cell mtDNA sequencing, long-read sequencing, digital droplet PCR, and spatial approaches linking mutation burden to histopathology.

Rationale: I’ll read a representative source summary to refine the proposal around methodological gaps and translational relevance.

```action
{"tool":"read","source":"review summary on somatic mtDNA mutations and neurodegeneration"}
```

[result]: Key gaps include distinguishing causal mtDNA mutations from age-associated passenger mutations, resolving cell-type-specific heteroplasmy in vulnerable neuronal populations, and connecting mutation burden to functional phenotypes such as respiratory chain deficiency. Translational opportunities include mtDNA mutation biomarkers in blood, cerebrospinal fluid, or cell-free DNA; patient-derived iPSC models for therapeutic screening; and interventions targeting mitochondrial biogenesis, mitophagy, nucleotide balance, or selective reduction of pathogenic heteroplasmy.

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"This project will investigate how age-associated mitochondrial DNA mutations contribute to neurodegenerative disease, with a focus on Parkinson’s disease and Alzheimer’s disease as representative disorders involving neuronal energy failure, oxidative stress, and selective vulnerability. The central hypothesis is that somatic mtDNA point mutations and deletions accumulate in specific neuronal and glial populations during aging, exceed functional heteroplasmy thresholds in vulnerable circuits, and thereby promote respiratory chain dysfunction, inflammatory signaling, and neurodegenerative pathology.\n\nThe study will use complementary model systems. First, postmortem human brain tissue from aged controls and patients with neurodegenerative disease will be analyzed in disease-relevant regions such as substantia nigra, hippocampus, and cortex. Second, patient-derived induced pluripotent stem cells will be differentiated into dopaminergic neurons, cortical neurons, astrocytes, and microglia-like cells to test whether inherited nuclear background and acquired mtDNA variation interact to shape mitochondrial phenotypes. Third, mouse models with increased mtDNA mutational burden, including polymerase gamma mutator mice or targeted mitochondrial genome-editing models where feasible, will be used to assess temporal relationships between mtDNA mutation accumulation, neuronal dysfunction, and behavioral decline.\n\nMutation detection will combine high-sensitivity and spatially resolved methods. Duplex sequencing and ultra-deep mtDNA sequencing will quantify low-frequency heteroplasmic variants, while long-read sequencing and long-range PCR will detect large-scale deletions and rearrangements. Single-cell or single-nucleus mtDNA sequencing will identify cell-type-specific mutation burdens, and digital droplet PCR will validate recurrent deletions or pathogenic variants. These genomic data will be integrated with measurements of mitochondrial function, including oxygen consumption, respiratory chain enzymology, mitochondrial membrane potential, reactive oxygen species production, mitophagy markers, and single-cell transcriptomics. Spatial transcriptomic or histochemical assays for cytochrome c oxidase deficiency will help connect mutation burden to local tissue dysfunction.\n\nA major goal is to distinguish causal mutations from age-related passenger mutations. To do this, iPSC-derived neurons and cybrid cell lines carrying defined heteroplasmy levels will be used to test whether specific mtDNA variants impair respiration, increase vulnerability to proteotoxic stress, or trigger innate immune pathways. In animal models, longitudinal assessment of motor, cognitive, and neuropathological phenotypes will determine whether rising mtDNA mutation load precedes or follows neurodegeneration. Computational modeling will estimate heteroplasmy thresholds for dysfunction across cell types and identify mutation signatures associated with disease rather than chronological aging alone.\n\nThe translational relevance of this work lies in biomarker and therapeutic development. If disease-associated mtDNA mutation patterns are detectable in cerebrospinal fluid, blood-derived cell-free DNA, or peripheral cells, they could support early diagnosis or patient stratification. Mechanistic findings may also guide interventions that enhance mitophagy, support mitochondrial biogenesis, improve nucleotide homeostasis, or selectively reduce pathogenic mtDNA heteroplasmy. Ultimately, this research could clarify whether mitochondrial genome instability is merely a marker of aging or an actionable driver of age-related neurodegenerative disease."}
```

topic: [v15/en/free_text_answer/biology] What experimental approaches could reveal how coral symbionts contribute to thermal tolerance during marine heatwaves?
full_prompt: What experimental approaches could reveal how coral symbionts contribute to thermal tolerance during marine heatwaves?
completion:
I’ll first scan for recent experimental themes linking coral algal symbionts, microbiomes, and thermal tolerance under marine heatwave conditions.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"experimental approaches coral symbionts thermal tolerance marine heatwaves Symbiodiniaceae microbiome reciprocal transplant heat stress"}
```

[result]: Recent studies emphasize controlled heat-stress experiments, Symbiodiniaceae community profiling, host–symbiont “shuffling” or inoculation, microbiome manipulation, reciprocal transplantation, multi-omics, and physiological assays such as photosynthetic efficiency, bleaching score, respiration, oxidative stress, and survival. Key recurring questions include whether thermotolerant Symbiodiniaceae types, bacteria, or host genotype drive persistence during acute marine heatwaves.

To focus the proposal, I’ll read one representative synthesis on coral symbiosis and heat tolerance.

```action
{"tool":"read","source":"representative review on coral holobiont thermal tolerance and Symbiodiniaceae/microbiome responses to heat stress"}
```

[result]: The review highlights that coral heat tolerance is an emergent holobiont trait involving host genetics, algal symbiont identity and function, bacterial communities, nutrient exchange, antioxidant capacity, and acclimatization history. It recommends manipulative experiments that separate host, algal symbiont, and bacterial effects, ideally under realistic fluctuating heatwave regimes rather than constant-temperature stress.

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"A strong experimental strategy would combine controlled marine heatwave simulations with symbiont manipulation to test causality rather than correlation. Coral fragments from multiple genotypes could be experimentally inoculated with different Symbiodiniaceae communities, such as thermally sensitive Cladocopium-dominated assemblages versus thermotolerant Durusdinium-dominated assemblages, then exposed to realistic heatwave profiles that include gradual warming, peak-temperature pulses, and recovery. Comparing bleaching severity, survival, photosynthetic efficiency, respiration, calcification, and oxidative damage across host genotype–symbiont combinations would reveal whether particular algal symbionts directly increase thermal tolerance.\n\nA second approach would be reciprocal transplant or common-garden experiments using corals from reefs with contrasting thermal histories. By placing corals and their native symbionts into shared temperature treatments, researchers could distinguish effects of prior acclimatization from fixed symbiont identity. Parallel sequencing of Symbiodiniaceae ITS2 or whole-genome markers before, during, and after heat stress would show whether tolerant colonies maintain their original symbionts, shift toward heat-tolerant types, or recover through symbiont community restructuring.\n\nThe bacterial microbiome should also be tested experimentally. Antibiotic-treated corals, microbiome-transplanted corals, and corals inoculated with candidate beneficial bacteria could be exposed to identical heatwave treatments. If certain bacterial consortia reduce bleaching, enhance antioxidant defenses, stabilize nutrient cycling, or improve post-heatwave recovery, this would indicate that non-algal symbionts contribute to thermal resilience as part of the coral holobiont.\n\nFinally, multi-omics and isotope-tracing approaches could reveal mechanism. Transcriptomics, proteomics, metabolomics, and stable carbon or nitrogen isotope labeling during heat stress would show how symbionts alter photosynthate transfer, nitrogen recycling, reactive oxygen species control, and host stress-response pathways. The most informative design would integrate manipulation, time-series sampling, and field validation, allowing researchers to identify not only which symbionts are associated with heat tolerance, but how they mechanistically help corals survive marine heatwaves."}
```

topic: [v15/en/free_text_answer/biology] Write a free-text research proposal investigating how gut microbiome diversity influences immune checkpoint inhibitor response in patients with melanoma. Includ
full_prompt: Write a free-text research proposal investigating how gut microbiome diversity influences immune checkpoint inhibitor response in patients with melanoma. Include hypothesis, experimental design, expected outcomes, and potential limitations.
completion:
I’ll first search for recent evidence connecting gut microbiome composition/diversity with immune checkpoint inhibitor outcomes in melanoma, then use that to frame a focused proposal.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"gut microbiome diversity immune checkpoint inhibitor response melanoma patients anti-PD-1 clinical study"}
```

[result]: Studies in melanoma patients treated with anti-PD-1/anti-CTLA-4 therapies report associations between gut microbiome diversity and clinical response. Responders often show higher alpha diversity and enrichment of taxa such as Ruminococcaceae, Faecalibacterium, Bifidobacterium, and Akkermansia, while non-responders may show enrichment of Bacteroidales or lower-diversity communities. Fecal microbiota transplantation from responders has been explored in refractory melanoma patients, with evidence of immune modulation and occasional clinical benefit.

To refine the proposal, I’ll read a representative clinical-translational study summary on microbiome features and checkpoint blockade response.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"Gopalakrishnan gut microbiome modulates response anti-PD-1 melanoma Science 2018 summary"}
```

[result]: The study found that melanoma patients responding to anti-PD-1 therapy had significantly higher gut microbiome diversity and relative enrichment of Ruminococcaceae/Faecalibacterium. Responders also showed enhanced systemic and intratumoral antitumor immunity, including increased antigen presentation and T-cell infiltration. Germ-free mice receiving fecal microbiota from responding patients showed improved tumor control and stronger anti-PD-L1 responses compared with mice receiving microbiota from non-responders.

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"This project will investigate how gut microbiome diversity influences response to immune checkpoint inhibitors in patients with melanoma. The central hypothesis is that higher baseline gut microbiome diversity, together with enrichment of specific immunomodulatory bacterial taxa, promotes stronger systemic and intratumoral antitumor immunity and thereby improves clinical response to anti-PD-1–based therapy. Conversely, low-diversity or dysbiotic gut communities may be associated with impaired T-cell activation, reduced tumor immune infiltration, and resistance to treatment.\n\nWe will conduct a prospective observational study of patients with advanced melanoma initiating standard-of-care immune checkpoint blockade, including anti-PD-1 monotherapy or anti-PD-1/anti-CTLA-4 combination therapy. Stool samples will be collected at baseline, early on-treatment, and at first radiographic assessment. Microbiome diversity and composition will be measured using shotgun metagenomic sequencing, with 16S rRNA sequencing as a lower-cost complementary approach. Clinical outcomes will include objective response rate, progression-free survival, overall survival, immune-related adverse events, antibiotic exposure, diet, proton-pump inhibitor use, and other relevant confounders.\n\nTo connect microbiome patterns with immune mechanisms, paired blood and, when available, tumor biopsy samples will be analyzed. Peripheral immune profiling will include flow cytometry or single-cell RNA sequencing to quantify activated CD8+ T cells, regulatory T cells, myeloid populations, and inflammatory cytokine signatures. Tumor samples will be assessed for T-cell infiltration, antigen-presentation markers, PD-L1 expression, and interferon-gamma–related gene expression. Statistical models will test whether baseline alpha diversity, beta-diversity clustering, and abundance of candidate taxa such as Faecalibacterium, Ruminococcaceae, Bifidobacterium, or Akkermansia predict checkpoint inhibitor response independently of clinical covariates.\n\nExpected outcomes are that responders will show greater baseline microbial diversity and distinct functional microbial pathways related to short-chain fatty acid production, mucosal immune regulation, or antigen presentation. We also expect that favorable microbiome profiles will correlate with increased circulating and intratumoral effector T-cell activity. These findings could support development of microbiome-based biomarkers to stratify melanoma patients before immunotherapy and may identify rational candidates for microbiome-modulating interventions, such as dietary fiber optimization, targeted probiotics, or fecal microbiota transplantation.\n\nPotential limitations include the observational nature of the study, which may identify associations but not prove causality. Microbiome composition is influenced by diet, geography, medications, prior antibiotics, host genetics, and tumor burden, all of which may confound response analyses. Sample size may limit detection of modest taxonomic effects, and different sequencing or bioinformatic pipelines can produce variable results. Finally, microbiome signatures may not generalize across treatment regimens or patient populations, so validation in independent cohorts and mechanistic testing in preclinical models would be necessary before clinical translation."}
```

[result]: 代表性思路显示：可将短时人群预测输出为概率占据区域或多模态轨迹分布，再通过风险约束、机会约束或控制屏障函数转化为控制限制。MPC适合表达舒适距离、目标跟踪和能耗等多目标，但需要降维、候选轨迹采样或GPU并行来满足实时性。CBF-QP具有快速反应和安全过滤优势，但单独使用时对社会规范表达不足。

基于检索结果，提出一个将“社会规范约束、概率预测与实时安全过滤”统一起来的控制研究方向。  
```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"建议研究方向为：面向动态人群导航的预测感知社会规范约束实时控制框架。核心思想是把移动机器人导航分成两层：上层利用人群交互预测模型生成未来数秒内的多模态人类轨迹分布，下层采用带社会规范约束的模型预测控制或采样式MPC生成候选动作，并由控制屏障函数安全过滤器进行最后一层实时可行性保证。\n\n在预测模型方面，可采用图神经网络或Transformer式交互预测器，将行人位置、速度、朝向、群体关系、通行方向和场景语义编码为时空图，输出多模态轨迹及不确定性。与其只预测单条最可能轨迹，不如输出概率占据管或若干行为意图模式，例如直行、减速、绕行、横穿。控制器随后根据预测分布进行风险约束规划，而不是对人群未来位置做确定性假设。\n\n在社会规范约束方面，可将个人空间、右侧通行、避免切入群体、礼让迎面行人、降低从背后高速接近等规则形式化为可微约束或风险代价。其中关键研究点是区分硬安全约束与软社会规范约束：碰撞避免和最小安全距离由机会约束或CBF保证；舒适距离、让行偏好和群体完整性作为可调权重的社会代价进入MPC。进一步可设计社会规范控制屏障函数，使机器人不仅不碰撞，而且避免进入高社会风险区域。\n\n在实时控制可行性方面，可采用分层求解：先用学习模型快速给出局部可行走廊或候选速度集，再用短时域MPC/MPPI优化速度指令，最后通过低维CBF-QP在毫秒级修正控制输入。为了降低计算负担，可只对机器人周围最相关的若干行人建立约束，并用概率占据椭圆或多边形近似人群预测结果。这样既保留预测模型对复杂交互的表达能力，又避免端到端策略在安全性和可解释性上的不足。\n\n该方向的实验验证可包括仿真与真实机器人两部分：在不同密度、交叉流、排队、窄通道和群体同行场景下，比较传统MPC、纯学习导航、CBF避障和所提框架的碰撞率、到达时间、社会舒适度、最小人机距离、控制频率和计算延迟。预期贡献是形成一种可解释、可调参、具备概率安全保证且满足实时运行需求的社会导航控制方法。"}
```

full_prompt: 请撰写一份关于四足机器人在复杂地形上进行鲁棒运动控制的自由文本研究提案，重点讨论模型预测控制与强化学习的融合方案。
completion:
我将先检索该方向的代表性进展，尤其关注 MPC、RL 以及二者在四足机器人复杂地形运动控制中的结合方式，以便形成一个聚焦且可执行的研究提案。  

[result]: 近期研究显示，四足机器人复杂地形运动控制主要有三类路线：基于质心动力学和接触规划的模型预测控制、基于仿真大规模训练的强化学习策略，以及将 MPC 的可解释约束优化能力与 RL 的残差补偿、地形适应或参数调节能力结合的混合方法。典型挑战包括接触不确定性、地形高度感知误差、足端打滑、执行器延迟、模型失配以及真实部署中的 sim-to-real 差距。

为进一步明确融合方案的技术切入点，我将读取一篇代表性综述/论文摘要级信息，提炼适合研究提案的系统架构。  
```action
{"tool":"read","source":"Representative papers on legged locomotion MPC-RL: convex MPC for quadrupeds, learning-based residual control, perceptive locomotion with reinforcement learning"}
```
[result]: 代表性工作表明，MPC 通常使用简化质心模型在线优化地面反力、步态相位和机体姿态，具有约束处理和稳定性优势；强化学习可用于学习低层关节控制、复杂地形下的落足策略、模型误差补偿或 MPC 代价函数自适应调节。融合架构常见形式包括：RL 生成高层速度/落足目标、MPC 负责动力学可行轨迹；MPC 生成参考，RL 学习残差；以及 RL 在线调节 MPC 权重、摩擦参数和安全裕度。

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"本研究拟面向四足机器人在碎石、台阶、斜坡、松软地面和低附着表面等复杂地形上的鲁棒运动控制，提出一种“模型预测控制—强化学习”融合框架。核心思想是利用模型预测控制在动力学约束、接触力约束和稳定性保证方面的优势，同时引入强化学习对模型误差、地形不确定性和执行器非理想因素进行自适应补偿，从而兼顾可解释性、安全性与环境适应能力。\n\n具体而言，系统将采用分层控制结构：高层感知模块基于深度相机或激光雷达构建局部地形高度图，并估计可通行性、坡度、摩擦风险与潜在落足区域；中层 MPC 基于质心动力学模型和接触序列，在滚动时域内优化机体速度、姿态、足端接触力和参考落足点；低层控制器则跟踪 MPC 产生的期望轨迹。强化学习模块不直接完全替代 MPC，而是以残差控制、参数自适应和策略先验的形式参与：一方面学习对足端力、机体姿态和摆腿轨迹的残差修正，另一方面根据地形特征和机器人状态在线调节 MPC 代价权重、摩擦锥裕度、步长和步高等关键参数。\n\n研究的关键问题包括三点。第一，如何在保证安全约束的条件下引入学习策略，避免纯强化学习在真实机器人上可能出现的不稳定行为；本研究将采用安全过滤器或控制屏障函数，对 RL 输出进行可行性投影，使其不违反接触力、关节力矩、足端碰撞和机体姿态约束。第二，如何解决仿真到现实迁移问题；计划在仿真中进行动力学随机化、地形随机化和传感噪声注入，并结合少量真实数据进行残差模型微调。第三，如何在实时性与性能之间取得平衡；MPC 将使用简化动力学和快速二次规划求解器，RL 模块采用轻量神经网络，仅输出低维残差或参数修正，以满足百赫兹级控制频率需求。\n\n实验评估将分为仿真和实机两阶段。在仿真环境中，将构建多类型复杂地形，包括随机高度场、连续台阶、斜坡、低摩擦区域和外力扰动场景，并与纯 MPC、纯 RL 以及固定参数 MPC 等基线方法比较。指标包括最大可通过坡度、跌倒率、速度跟踪误差、能耗、足端打滑率、姿态扰动恢复时间和控制计算延迟。在实机平台上，将选择中小型四足机器人进行验证，重点测试未知地形上的稳定通行、突发推搡恢复、负载变化适应和低附着地面行走能力。\n\n预期贡献包括：提出一种具有安全约束的 MPC-RL 融合控制架构；建立面向复杂地形的 MPC 参数自适应与残差学习方法；形成一套从仿真训练到真实部署的鲁棒迁移流程；并通过系统实验揭示模型驱动控制与数据驱动学习在四足机器人运动控制中的互补机制。该研究有望提升四足机器人在野外巡检、灾害救援、矿区勘探和城市复杂环境中的自主移动能力。"}
```

[result]: 相关研究显示：极端高温显著增加老年人热相关疾病、心血管与呼吸系统急症、死亡率及住院风险；风险在独居、低收入、慢性病患者和居住于热岛效应强、绿地不足、步行可达避暑空间缺乏的社区中更高。城市规划研究强调绿地、树荫、凉爽路面、通风廊道、公共避暑中心与邻里可达性对降低热暴露具有重要作用，但公共卫生风险评估与社区空间设计之间仍缺少精细化整合。

```action

```

[result]: 文献综述表明，当前研究多分别评估“热暴露—健康结果”或“城市形态—热环境”，较少把老年人的日常活动路径、社区设施可达性、社会孤立程度和微气候环境纳入同一分析框架。一个有潜力的方向是构建“老年人热风险—空间脆弱性”模型，并将其转化为社区更新和适应性规划策略。

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"本研究可聚焦于“极端高温背景下老年人健康风险的社区空间机制与适应性规划策略”。核心问题是：在气候变化导致热浪频率、强度和持续时间增加的背景下，哪些社区空间特征会放大或缓解老年人的健康风险？研究对象可选择高温事件频发的大城市或老龄化程度较高的城区，重点比较不同类型社区，如高密度老旧小区、低绿化率社区、城郊安置社区与设施完善的宜居社区。\n\n研究将结合公共卫生、城市气候学和城市规划方法。首先，利用气象数据、地表温度遥感数据、热浪记录和医疗急救或住院数据，识别极端高温期间老年人健康风险的空间分布。其次，将社区层面的绿地覆盖率、树荫连续性、建筑密度、道路硬化比例、通风条件、公共避暑空间可达性、医疗服务距离以及步行环境等指标纳入分析，构建“热暴露—空间脆弱性—健康结果”的综合模型。\n\n该研究尤其应关注老年人的行为与社会脆弱性。极端高温并不只是一个环境暴露问题，也与老年人是否独居、是否患有慢性病、是否能够负担空调费用、是否愿意或能够到达社区避暑空间有关。因此，可通过问卷、访谈或活动日志收集老年人的日常出行路径、避暑行为、风险感知和邻里互助网络信息，从而解释为什么同样的高温暴露在不同社区会导致不同健康后果。\n\n预期成果包括一套面向社区尺度的老年人极端高温健康风险地图，以及若干可转化为规划实践的设计策略。例如，在高风险社区优先增加连续树荫与口袋公园，优化老年人步行到达避暑中心的路径；在老旧小区更新中引入凉爽屋顶、透水与高反照率铺装、遮阳廊架和通风开敞空间；同时将社区卫生服务站、养老服务设施和公共避暑点纳入热浪应急网络。该方向的意义在于把气候适应、健康公平与社区更新结合起来，为老龄化城市应对极端高温提供更精细和更公平的规划依据。"}
```

[result]: 模拟阅读结果显示：高分辨率卫星影像、无人机影像和植被指数可较好估计地块级作物长势、收获期产量和管理差异；但小农户地块破碎、作物混种和云覆盖会增加识别误差。将遥感数据与农户投入记录、市场价格、土壤信息和天气数据融合，可改进产量估计和环境足迹核算，并支持准实验或随机推广项目的影响评估。

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"本研究拟提出的问题是：精准农业服务是否能够同时提高小农户的作物产量与农业收入，并降低单位产出的生态足迹？具体而言，研究将评估基于遥感监测的精准施肥、精准灌溉和病虫害预警服务，对小农户地块级产量、农户净收入、投入使用效率以及碳排放、水足迹和氮素流失风险的综合影响。\n\n研究对象可选择一个小农户占比较高、作物相对集中的农业区域，例如水稻、小麦或玉米主产区。研究设计上，可与地方农业推广机构合作，将部分村庄或农户随机纳入精准农业服务组，另一部分作为对照组；若随机化不可行，则利用分阶段推广形成双重差分设计。遥感数据将用于持续监测地块植被指数、作物长势、灌溉状况和收获期产量，并与农户调查中的投入成本、劳动力、销售价格、补贴、技术采纳行为等经济数据相匹配。\n\n核心结果变量包括三类：第一，农业科学维度的单产、肥料利用效率、灌溉效率和病虫害损失；第二，经济学维度的净收入、收益波动、投入回报率和技术采纳成本；第三，生态维度的单位产量温室气体排放、用水量、氮肥盈余和潜在面源污染风险。通过将遥感反演产量与地面抽样测产校准，可以降低小农户自报产量的误差；通过生命周期评价或部分生态足迹核算，可以估计不同管理方式下的环境代价。\n\n本研究的主要贡献在于，把精准农业从单一“增产技术”扩展为一个产量—收入—生态协同评估问题。它不仅能回答精准农业是否对小农户有经济吸引力，还能识别其是否真正降低了单位农产品的环境压力。进一步地，研究可分析哪些农户受益最多，例如土地规模较大者、数字素养较高者、灌溉条件较好者，或原本投入使用效率较低者，从而为精准农业补贴、数字农业基础设施建设和绿色农业政策提供依据。"}
```

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"soft robot deformation modeling closed-loop control physics-informed data-driven model learning review"}
```

[result]: 近年研究集中在三类路线：1）基于 Cosserat rod、有限元、PCC/PCS 等低维物理模型进行软体机器人形变与动力学建模；2）使用神经网络、高斯过程、Koopman 表示、Neural ODE 等数据驱动模型学习输入—形变—力学响应；3）将物理约束、能量函数、材料本构、几何连续性和可观测性约束嵌入学习模型，用于提高样本效率、泛化能力和闭环控制稳定性。闭环控制方面常见方法包括模型预测控制、视觉/形状反馈控制、自适应控制和强化学习控制。

基于上述脉络，进一步读取一篇综述/代表性资料以提炼可行研究切入点。

```action
{"tool":"read","source":"representative survey on soft robot modeling and control with physics-informed learning"}
```

[result]: 资料指出，软体机器人控制的核心瓶颈在于：高维连续形变难以实时建模，材料非线性、迟滞、接触和流固耦合导致模型失配；纯物理模型可解释但参数辨识困难，纯数据模型灵活但外推和稳定性不足。一个有前景的方向是构建“物理结构化的可学习状态空间模型”，在低维形状坐标中嵌入连续介质力学、能量耗散、输入约束与传感器观测模型，并结合 MPC 或控制 Lyapunov/Barrier 函数实现安全闭环控制。

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"本研究拟面向气动、腱驱或流体驱动软体机器人，研究一种融合数据驱动模型与物理先验的形变建模及闭环控制框架。软体机器人具有连续体、大形变、强非线性和材料迟滞等特征，传统刚体机器人控制理论难以直接适用；而单纯依赖有限元或 Cosserat rod 等物理模型往往计算代价高、参数辨识困难，难以满足实时控制需求。因此，本项目的核心目标是建立可实时预测、可解释、可控制且具备一定泛化能力的软体机器人动态形变模型，并在此基础上实现稳定、鲁棒的闭环控制。\n\n在建模方面，本研究计划采用“低维物理坐标 + 可学习残差动力学”的混合建模策略。首先基于分段常曲率、Cosserat rod 或模态分解方法，将软体机器人连续形变映射到低维形状变量，例如曲率、弯曲平面、伸长量或主模态系数；随后引入神经状态空间模型、Neural ODE、Koopman 表示或高斯过程模型学习未建模动态，包括材料迟滞、摩擦、流体延迟、接触扰动和执行器非理想性。与纯黑箱模型不同，学习模块将受到几何连续性、应变能、阻尼耗散、输入饱和和边界条件等物理先验约束，从而提升样本效率和外推稳定性。\n\n在感知与状态估计方面，本研究将融合视觉、嵌入式应变传感器、IMU、压力传感器或光纤传感器数据，构建软体机器人形状状态的在线估计器。考虑到软体系统通常存在部分可观测和传感噪声问题，可采用物理先验约束的扩展卡尔曼滤波、因子图优化或学习型观测器，将传感器测量映射到低维形变状态。进一步地，观测模型也可与动力学模型联合训练，使模型不仅能预测执行器输入下的形变演化，还能根据实时传感反馈修正模型误差。\n\n在控制方面，本研究拟基于所学混合模型设计闭环模型预测控制框架。控制器在滚动时域内利用物理结构化模型预测未来形变，并优化执行器压力、腱张力或流量输入以跟踪目标轨迹、保持姿态或完成抓取任务。为提高安全性和稳定性，可在 MPC 中加入控制 Lyapunov 函数、控制 Barrier 函数、能量约束或可达集约束，限制过大应变、执行器饱和和接触力风险。同时，可研究自适应更新机制，使模型在不同负载、材料老化或环境接触条件下逐步校正参数。\n\n预期贡献包括：提出一种兼顾物理可解释性与数据适应性的软体机器人动态建模方法；建立面向闭环控制的低维实时形变预测模型；发展融合视觉/触觉/本体传感的在线状态估计与模型校正算法；并在轨迹跟踪、软体抓取或受限空间运动等任务中验证该方法相对于纯物理模型和纯数据模型在样本效率、控制精度、鲁棒性和泛化能力上的优势。该研究有望为软体机器人从开环演示走向可靠闭环自主操作提供基础控制方法。"}
```

topic: [v15/zh/free_text_answer/interdisciplinary] 请撰写一份自由文本研究提案，探讨脑机接口、康复医学与伦理学交叉领域中，运动障碍患者辅助技术的可及性与责任边界。
full_prompt: 请撰写一份自由文本研究提案，探讨脑机接口、康复医学与伦理学交叉领域中，运动障碍患者辅助技术的可及性与责任边界。
completion:

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"brain-computer interface rehabilitation medicine ethics accessibility responsibility motor disorders assistive technology"}
```

[result]: 相关研究显示，脑机接口（BCI）与康复机器人、功能性电刺激、智能假肢等技术正在用于卒中、脊髓损伤、帕金森病、肌萎缩侧索硬化症等运动障碍患者的功能恢复或辅助沟通。主要争议包括：高成本与地域不平等、患者知情同意与长期数据治理、算法误差导致的伤害责任、临床医生与工程团队之间的责任边界，以及商业化设备进入家庭场景后的监管缺口。

为细化研究框架，我将读取一条代表性材料，提炼其对“临床康复—技术部署—伦理治理”的启示。

```action
{"tool":"read","source":"Representative review on neurotechnology ethics, rehabilitation BCI, and assistive device governance"}
```

[result]: 文献强调，运动障碍辅助技术不应仅以功能增益作为评价标准，还应纳入患者自主性、尊严、社会参与、照护负担、长期可维护性与公平分配。BCI 系统涉及神经数据采集、机器学习解释、设备调参和人机共同决策，因此责任不能简单归属于患者、医生或制造商，而应建立分层责任模型，包括设计责任、临床适配责任、使用监督责任和制度保障责任。

基于上述发现，我将提出一份跨学科研究提案，围绕可及性与责任边界构建实证—规范结合的研究路径。

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"本研究拟探讨脑机接口、康复医学与伦理学交叉领域中，运动障碍患者辅助技术的可及性与责任边界。随着侵入式或非侵入式脑机接口、康复机器人、智能假肢和功能性电刺激系统逐渐从实验室走向临床与家庭，运动障碍患者获得沟通、移动和自我照护能力的可能性显著增加。然而，这类技术也可能因价格高昂、训练资源稀缺、地区医疗差异、算法不透明和长期维护不足而加剧既有不平等。因此，本研究的核心问题是：谁能够真实获得这些技术，谁在技术失败或伤害发生时承担责任，以及如何在创新、康复收益与患者权利之间建立可持续平衡。\n\n研究将采用跨学科混合方法。第一部分通过临床场景调研，比较不同运动障碍患者群体在脑机接口及相关辅助技术获取过程中的障碍，包括经济支付能力、医保覆盖、医院康复资源、数字素养、家庭照护条件和城乡差异。第二部分将访谈患者、康复医师、工程师、护理人员、伦理委员会成员和设备企业代表，分析各方如何理解“有效康复”“自主选择”“合理风险”和“技术责任”。第三部分将选取若干典型使用场景，例如卒中后上肢康复训练、脊髓损伤患者外骨骼辅助行走、ALS 患者脑机接口沟通系统，建立责任链条图谱，识别设计、验证、临床适配、数据处理、日常使用和故障应对中的关键责任节点。\n\n本研究的理论贡献在于提出一种“分层责任—情境可及性”框架。可及性不仅被理解为设备是否存在或是否可支付，还包括患者是否能够获得持续训练、技术解释、维修支持、心理适应和社会参与机会。责任边界也不应被简化为事故后的法律归责，而应扩展为全过程责任：研发者需承担安全性、可解释性和偏差控制责任；临床团队需承担适应证评估、风险沟通和康复目标协调责任；企业需承担售后维护、数据保护和真实效果披露责任；医疗制度与公共政策则需承担公平分配、支付支持和监管更新责任。\n\n预期成果包括三类：一是形成运动障碍辅助技术可及性评估指标，帮助医院和政策制定者识别哪些患者被技术转化过程排除在外；二是提出脑机接口康复应用的责任分配模型，为临床伦理审查、知情同意文件和不良事件处理提供参考；三是制定面向患者的参与式治理建议，确保患者不是被动接受技术的对象，而是共同定义康复目标、风险阈值和生活质量标准的主体。最终，本研究旨在推动脑机接口辅助技术从“高端创新展示”转向“负责任、可负担、可持续并尊重患者尊严的康复实践”。"}
```

topic: [v15/en/free_text_answer/biology] Propose a research project examining whether CRISPR-based gene drives could be safely used to control invasive mosquito populations. Discuss ecological risks an
full_prompt: Propose a research project examining whether CRISPR-based gene drives could be safely used to control invasive mosquito populations. Discuss ecological risks and containment strategies.
completion:
Rationale: I will first gather high-level context on current gene-drive risk assessment frameworks and containment strategies, then use that to frame a safe, non-operational research proposal focused on ecological evaluation rather than drive construction.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"CRISPR gene drive mosquitoes ecological risk assessment containment strategies invasive mosquito control"}
```

[result]: Recent reviews emphasize that gene-drive mosquito research should proceed through phased testing, ecological modeling, molecular confinement, geographic isolation, community engagement, and regulatory review. Key concerns include spread beyond target populations, effects on food webs, hybridization with non-target species, resistance evolution, and governance across political boundaries.

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"This project would examine whether CRISPR-based gene drives could be safely considered as a tool for controlling invasive mosquito populations, focusing on ecological risk assessment and containment rather than constructing or releasing drive-bearing organisms. The central research question would be: under what ecological, genetic, and governance conditions could a gene drive aimed at an invasive mosquito be expected to reduce disease or nuisance burden while minimizing unintended environmental effects? The study would combine population genetics, ecological network analysis, and stakeholder-informed risk assessment.\n\nFirst, the project would develop spatially explicit models of invasive mosquito population dynamics in a defined island or otherwise geographically bounded setting. These models would compare hypothetical gene-drive strategies, such as population suppression versus population replacement, against non-drive controls including habitat management, sterile insect techniques, and conventional vector control. The modeling would examine drive spread, loss of efficacy through resistance, seasonal population rebounds, and the probability of dispersal beyond the target region. Particular attention would be given to uncertainty, including migration rates, mating structure, climate variability, and the possibility of hybridization with closely related mosquito species.\n\nSecond, the project would assess ecological risks associated with reducing or altering invasive mosquito populations. Although many invasive mosquitoes are not keystone species, their removal could still affect predators, competitors, parasites, or pollination interactions in some ecosystems. The research would use field surveys, diet metabarcoding data from mosquito predators, and ecological network modeling to estimate whether suppression could produce measurable cascading effects. It would also evaluate risks of niche replacement, where removal of one invasive mosquito might allow another vector species to expand.\n\nThird, the project would evaluate containment and governance strategies appropriate for any future phased testing. Containment approaches to be assessed would include molecular confinement concepts such as self-limiting or threshold-dependent drive systems, ecological confinement through geographically isolated trial sites, physical containment in laboratory and cage studies, and operational safeguards such as reproductive barriers, monitoring zones, and predefined halt criteria. The project would also examine post-release surveillance requirements, including environmental DNA sampling, mosquito genomic monitoring, and community reporting systems.\n\nThe expected outcome would be a decision framework for determining whether CRISPR-based gene drives should advance beyond laboratory study for a particular invasive mosquito system. Rather than assuming gene drives are inherently safe or unsafe, the project would identify measurable safety benchmarks: low probability of transboundary spread, minimal predicted ecological disruption, manageable resistance evolution, robust containment options, and meaningful public and regulatory oversight. This would provide policymakers and biologists with a transparent basis for comparing gene drives with existing mosquito control methods before any environmental deployment is considered."}
```

[result]: 文献综述指出，社区微电网的成功不只取决于技术配置，还取决于治理安排。合作社模式、地方政府主导模式、公私合营模式和公用事业公司主导模式在资金来源、风险分配、决策透明度和居民参与程度上差异明显。研究建议将停电恢复时间、关键负荷保障率、可再生能源自给率等工程指标，与参与广度、决策影响力、收益分配公平性、能源负担变化等社会指标结合评估。

```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"研究主题可设定为：社区微电网如何通过“技术韧性—政策治理—公众参与”的协同机制提升城市与乡村社区的能源韧性。该研究将社区微电网视为一种社会技术系统，而不仅是分布式能源工程项目，重点分析其在极端天气、公共安全事件或主电网故障情境下，如何保障关键负荷、缩短停电时间，并同时激发居民参与能源决策。\n\n在能源工程层面，研究可构建微电网韧性评估框架，考察光伏、储能、可控负荷、电动汽车和智能控制系统的配置对供能可靠性的影响。核心指标包括孤岛运行时长、关键设施供电保障率、恢复时间、峰谷负荷调节能力和可再生能源消纳率。通过情景模拟，可比较不同技术组合在灾害冲击下的表现。\n\n在政策科学层面，研究应关注不同治理和激励机制如何影响微电网部署。可比较政府补贴、公私合营、社区能源合作社、电价激励和韧性服务补偿等政策工具，分析其对投资可行性、收益分配和长期运维责任的影响。特别需要探讨现有电力监管制度是否允许社区主体参与能源交易、需求响应和本地能源治理。\n\n在社会学层面，研究将公众参与作为核心变量，考察居民是否只是微电网服务的消费者，还是能够参与规划、投资、监督和收益分配的共同治理者。可通过问卷、访谈和参与式观察分析社区信任、风险感知、能源公平、弱势群体可及性以及地方认同对项目接受度的影响。研究假设是：参与程度越高、收益分配越透明，社区微电网越可能获得持续支持，并在危机中形成更强的互助网络。\n\n该研究的贡献在于提出一个跨学科评估模型，将工程韧性指标、政策工具效果和社会参与质量整合起来。最终可为地方政府、能源企业和社区组织提供政策建议：社区微电网不应仅被设计为备用电源，而应被建设为提升能源韧性、推动能源民主和促进社区协作的公共基础设施。"}
```

[result]: 阅读结果显示，可行实验范式包括：构建模拟短视频平台并操纵推荐逻辑，如“情绪一致推荐”“外貌/成就比较内容推荐”“多样化推荐”“随机推荐”；心理指标可包括PANAS情绪量表、Rosenberg自尊量表、Erikson身份认同量表、状态焦虑量表，以及观看过程中的停留时长、重看、跳过、点赞等行为特征。伦理上需限制负面内容强度，设置退出机制、家长同意、事后心理支持与去欺骗说明。

基于上述线索，我将提出一个兼具实验控制、生态有效性和数据科学建模的跨学科方案。  
```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"本研究拟设计一个融合心理学、社交媒体研究与数据科学的混合实验，考察短视频推荐算法如何影响青少年的情绪波动与自我认同。研究对象为13—17岁青少年，采用家长知情同意与青少年本人同意的双重伦理流程。实验将搭建一个受控的模拟短视频平台，内容来自经过审核的中性、积极、轻度负面、外貌比较、学业/成就比较、同伴生活方式等视频素材库，避免真实平台中不可控的极端内容。\n\n实验采用随机分组设计：第一组接受“情绪一致型推荐”，即系统根据被试早期观看偏好持续推送相似情绪色彩内容；第二组接受“社会比较强化型推荐”，更多推送外貌、成就、生活方式等易诱发比较的内容；第三组接受“多样化保护型推荐”，系统有意混合不同主题与情绪强度，降低内容同质化；第四组为随机推荐对照组。每名被试连续7天每天使用模拟平台15—20分钟，并在使用前、使用后以及睡前完成简短生态瞬时评估，记录即时情绪、自尊、自我清晰度、社会比较感和归属感。\n\n心理学测量方面，研究将结合PANAS正负性情绪量表、状态焦虑量表、Rosenberg自尊量表、自我概念清晰度量表和青少年身份认同发展量表。社交媒体行为层面记录停留时长、滑走速度、重看、点赞、收藏、主动搜索、内容序列情绪强度等指标。数据科学部分将使用时间序列分析和多层线性模型估计算法条件对个体内情绪波动的影响，并使用机器学习模型预测哪些行为模式预示更大的情绪不稳定或自我认同下降。\n\n核心假设包括：第一，情绪一致型推荐会增强情绪惯性，使负面情绪恢复速度变慢；第二，社会比较强化型推荐会降低自尊和自我概念清晰度，尤其对原本自尊较低或社会比较倾向较高的青少年影响更强；第三，多样化保护型推荐可缓冲短视频沉浸浏览带来的情绪极化，并减少单一身份模板对青少年自我认同的塑形压力。研究还将检验性别、年龄、基线心理健康、家庭支持和日常平台使用强度的调节作用。\n\n本方案的贡献在于，它不只评估“短视频使用时长”与心理健康之间的相关性，而是直接操纵推荐算法逻辑，识别不同推荐机制的心理后果。研究结果可为青少年友好型推荐系统提供证据，例如引入内容多样性阈值、负面情绪连续暴露上限、社会比较内容提示、休息干预和个性化心理保护参数。伦理上，实验全程设置内容安全审查、即时退出按钮、情绪风险预警和事后心理资源转介，以确保研究在保护青少年参与者的前提下开展。"}
```

我将先检索相关研究与可用语料，确认“灾害叙事—社会响应”这一问题在数字人文和历史语言处理中可操作的切入点。  
```action

```
[result]: 检索到三类相关资源与研究方向：1）古代灾害史研究常使用正史、编年体史书、碑铭、简牍与宗教文献，关注旱涝、地震、蝗灾、疫病及其政治解释；2）数字人文中已有历史地理信息系统、时空知识图谱、事件抽取和文本标注方法；3）计算语言学可用于古汉语/古典语料的命名实体识别、事件抽取、叙事框架识别、情感与因果关系分析，但针对“灾害叙事与社会响应模式”的跨语料比较研究仍不足。

为细化研究设计，我再读取一项与历史灾害文本建模相关的代表性资料，提炼可复用的方法框架。  
```action
{"tool":"read","source":"Digital approaches to historical disaster records: event extraction, spatial mapping, and narrative classification"}
```
[result]: 资料指出，历史灾害文本分析通常包括四个步骤：构建标准化灾害事件本体；抽取时间、地点、灾种、损失、行动者和响应措施；将事件映射到历史GIS；再结合人工校验与叙事分类。难点包括古代地名变迁、纪年换算、灾异观念与事实记录混杂、同一事件在不同文本中的重复记载，以及政治修辞对灾害描述的塑形作用。

基于上述检索与阅读结果，我提出一个跨数字人文、计算语言学与历史学的研究课题。  
```action
{"tool":"propose","answer":"本课题拟题为“灾异、秩序与救助：古代文本中灾害叙事及社会响应模式的计算历史研究”。研究将以古代中国正史、编年史、政书、出土文献与碑铭材料为核心语料，并选取少量古希腊罗马或两河流域灾害记载作为比较样本，考察旱灾、洪水、地震、蝗灾、疫病等事件如何被叙述、解释与制度化回应。核心问题包括：古代文本如何将自然灾害转化为政治、道德或宗教意义事件？不同灾种对应何种社会响应模式？国家、地方官员、宗教群体与民众在灾害叙事中分别扮演什么角色？\n\n方法上，课题将建立一个“古代灾害叙事—响应”标注体系，包含灾种、时间、地点、损失、行动者、解释框架、响应措施与叙事评价等字段。计算语言学部分将结合古汉语分词、命名实体识别、历史地名归一化、事件抽取、因果关系识别与叙事框架分类，构建可检索的灾害事件数据库和知识图谱。数字人文部分将把抽取出的灾害事件与历史GIS结合，展示灾害发生、文本传播和救助措施之间的时空关系。\n\n历史学解释将关注三类社会响应模式：第一，礼仪与象征性响应，如罪己诏、祈雨、祭祀、改元与赦免；第二，制度性响应，如赈济、减税、开仓、迁徙安置、水利修复与医疗措施；第三，归责性响应，如将灾害归因于君德失范、官员失职、天意警示或外部敌患。通过比较不同时期、不同文体和不同政治语境中的叙事差异，本课题将揭示灾害并非仅是自然事件，而是古代社会理解秩序、责任与合法性的关键媒介。\n\n预期成果包括一个开放的古代灾害文本标注语料库、一套面向古代文本的灾害事件抽取模型、交互式灾害叙事地图，以及一部解释灾害叙事与国家治理关系的研究专著。该课题的创新之处在于，它不只统计灾害发生频率，而是把“灾害如何被书写”与“社会如何被动员”结合起来，从而在数字人文、计算语言学和历史解释之间建立可验证、可复用的研究范式。"}
```

topic: [v15/zh/free_text_answer/robotics_control] 构思一个面向工业装配机器人的高精度力位混合控制研究方案，重点包括接触状态识别、误差补偿和实验验证。

completion:
为构思方案，先检索力位混合控制在工业装配中的关键问题与近期研究侧重点，聚焦接触识别、补偿和验证体系。  
