topic: [v15/zh/idea_plan/clinical_medicine] 拟开展一项比较不同抗凝策略在非瓣膜性房颤合并慢性肾病患者中安全性与有效性的真实世界研究。请提出研究问题、数据来源、分组方法、终点事件和混杂因素控制方案。
full_prompt: 拟开展一项比较不同抗凝策略在非瓣膜性房颤合并慢性肾病患者中安全性与有效性的真实世界研究。请提出研究问题、数据来源、分组方法、终点事件和混杂因素控制方案。
completion:
先检索现有证据与指南，明确非瓣膜性房颤合并慢性肾病患者中抗凝药物选择、肾功能分层和常用真实世界结局定义。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"nonvalvular atrial fibrillation chronic kidney disease anticoagulation real-world study DOAC warfarin outcomes bleeding stroke eGFR"}
```
[result]: 既往真实世界研究多比较 DOACs 与华法林在 CKD 患者中的卒中/系统性栓塞、重大出血和死亡风险；常按 eGFR 分层，如 CKD 3期、4期、5期/透析。常用混杂控制方法包括倾向评分匹配、逆概率加权、多变量 Cox 回归，并调整 CHA2DS2-VASc、HAS-BLED、肾功能、合并症和合并用药。

基于检索结果，进一步聚焦“研究设计可执行性”，提出一个利用电子病历/医保数据库开展的新使用者队列研究方案。  
```action
{"tool":"propose","idea_plan":{"Idea":"开展一项真实世界回顾性新使用者队列研究，比较不同抗凝策略在非瓣膜性房颤合并慢性肾病患者中的有效性与安全性。核心研究问题为：在非瓣膜性房颤合并CKD患者中，直接口服抗凝药DOACs相较于华法林或未抗凝治疗，是否可降低卒中/系统性栓塞风险，同时不增加重大出血风险；不同DOAC及不同肾功能分层下风险获益是否存在差异。","ImplementationSteps":{"1":"数据来源：使用多中心医院电子病历、处方记录、实验室检查、住院病案首页及医保理赔数据库。纳入年龄≥18岁、诊断为非瓣膜性房颤且有CKD证据的患者；CKD定义为至少两次eGFR<60 ml/min/1.73m²间隔≥90天，或存在CKD诊断编码。排除机械瓣、生物瓣术后早期、中重度二尖瓣狭窄、近期瓣膜手术、短期抗凝适应证如术后预防、随访资料不足者。","2":"分组方法：采用新使用者设计，以首次开具抗凝药日期为index date。主要分组包括：DOAC组、华法林组、未抗凝组；DOAC组可进一步分为阿哌沙班、利伐沙班、达比加群、艾多沙班。根据基线eGFR分层：CKD 3期，eGFR 30-59；CKD 4期，eGFR 15-29；CKD 5期或透析，eGFR<15或维持透析。另可按剂量是否符合说明书肾功能推荐分为标准剂量、减量合规、潜在剂量不足或过量。","3":"终点事件：主要有效性终点为缺血性卒中或系统性栓塞。主要安全性终点为重大出血，包括颅内出血、胃肠道出血、需住院或输血的出血。次要终点包括全因死亡、心血管死亡、心肌梗死、复合净临床获益终点，即卒中/系统性栓塞、重大出血和死亡的组合终点。采用诊断编码、影像学记录、住院记录和死亡登记进行事件判定。","4":"混杂因素控制：收集人口学因素、基线eGFR和尿白蛋白、CKD分期、CHA2DS2-VASc评分、HAS-BLED评分、既往卒中、出血史、高血压、糖尿病、心力衰竭、冠心病、外周血管病、肝病、贫血、血小板异常、恶性肿瘤、跌倒风险、透析状态、合并用药如抗血小板药、NSAIDs、PPI、胺碘酮、他汀，以及医疗利用指标。采用倾向评分匹配或逆概率治疗加权平衡基线差异，并结合多变量Cox比例风险模型估计HR；对死亡竞争风险可使用Fine-Gray模型；开展肾功能分层、剂量分层、不同DOAC亚组、as-treated和intention-to-treat敏感性分析。","5":"偏倚控制与质量控制：设置至少6-12个月基线清洗期以确认新使用者；避免不死时间偏倚，index date定义为首次处方日期；使用时间依赖暴露或停药宽限期处理治疗转换和依从性；进行阴性对照结局或E-value分析评估未测量混杂；对缺失实验室指标采用多重插补。"},"ImplementationOrder":["1-2","3","4-5"],"Dataset":"多中心真实世界数据库，包括医院电子病历、门急诊和住院诊断、药品处方/发药记录、实验室eGFR和血红蛋白等检查结果、医保理赔、透析登记及死亡登记。研究对象为非瓣膜性房颤合并CKD患者的新使用者队列。","EvaluationMetrics":{"effectiveness_metric":"缺血性卒中或系统性栓塞发生率及调整后HR。","safety_metric":"重大出血发生率及调整后HR，重点关注颅内出血和胃肠道出血。","net_clinical_benefit":"卒中/系统性栓塞、重大出血和全因死亡的复合终点。","balance_metric":"倾向评分处理后协变量标准化差异SMD，目标为<0.1。","robustness_metric":"分层分析、敏感性分析和竞争风险模型结果的一致性。"},"ExpectedOutcome":"预期DOACs总体上较华法林具有相似或更低的卒中/系统性栓塞风险，并可能降低颅内出血风险；但在重度CKD或透析患者中，不同DOAC和剂量策略的安全性差异可能更明显。研究将为非瓣膜性房颤合并CKD患者的个体化抗凝选择、肾功能分层用药和剂量优化提供真实世界证据。"}}
```

[result]: 代表性研究多采用治疗前粪便样本16S rRNA测序或宏基因组测序，结合RECIST疗效评价、PFS/OS和CTCAE分级irAE记录。已有信号包括Akkermansia、Bifidobacterium、Ruminococcaceae、Faecalibacterium等可能与较好疗效相关，而菌群低多样性、抗生素暴露及某些促炎菌群模式可能与疗效较差或毒性升高相关。主要局限包括样本量偏小、混杂因素控制不足、缺乏动态菌群变化分析，以及疗效与毒性预测模型尚未达到临床转化水平。

```action
{"tool":"propose","idea_plan":{"Idea":"本研究拟开展一项前瞻性观察性临床队列研究，系统探讨晚期非小细胞肺癌患者接受免疫检查点抑制剂治疗过程中，肠道菌群基线特征及动态变化与抗肿瘤疗效和免疫相关不良事件之间的关系。核心假设是：特定肠道菌群组成、菌群多样性及功能通路可影响宿主抗肿瘤免疫状态，从而预测PD-1/PD-L1抑制剂治疗获益；同时，某些菌群失衡模式可能增加免疫相关不良事件风险。研究将结合粪便宏基因组测序、外周血免疫表型、炎症因子检测和临床结局，建立兼具疗效预测和毒性风险分层价值的多模态模型。","ImplementationSteps":{"1":"研究设计与对象入组：纳入拟接受PD-1/PD-L1抑制剂单药或联合化疗/抗血管治疗的晚期NSCLC患者，记录病理类型、分期、PD-L1表达、驱动基因状态、既往治疗、抗生素/益生菌/糖皮质激素使用、饮食及合并症等混杂因素。","2":"样本采集与检测：在治疗前、治疗6周、12周及疾病进展或出现≥2级免疫相关不良事件时采集粪便和外周血。粪便样本进行宏基因组测序，分析α多样性、β多样性、差异菌种及代谢功能通路；外周血检测T细胞亚群、髓系细胞、细胞因子和炎症指标。","3":"临床疗效与安全性评估：依据RECIST 1.1评估客观缓解率、疾病控制率、无进展生存期和总生存期；依据CTCAE v5.0记录免疫相关不良事件的发生时间、类型、严重程度、治疗干预和转归。","4":"统计分析与模型构建：比较应答者与非应答者、发生irAE与未发生irAE患者的菌群差异；采用多变量Cox回归、logistic回归和机器学习方法整合菌群、免疫和临床变量，构建疗效预测模型和irAE风险预测模型，并通过交叉验证评估稳定性。","5":"机制探索与临床转化：分析与疗效或毒性相关菌群的功能通路，如短链脂肪酸代谢、胆汁酸代谢、脂多糖合成及色氨酸代谢，并探索其与外周免疫激活、炎症因子和肿瘤控制之间的关联，为后续菌群干预研究提供依据。"},"ImplementationOrder":["1-2","3-4","5"],"Dataset":"前瞻性收集的晚期NSCLC免疫治疗队列数据，包括临床基线资料、治疗方案、影像疗效、PFS/OS、CTCAE定义的免疫相关不良事件、纵向粪便宏基因组测序数据、外周血免疫表型和炎症因子数据。","EvaluationMetrics":{"ORR":"客观缓解率，用于评价肠道菌群特征与肿瘤缩小疗效的关系。","DCR":"疾病控制率，用于评价菌群特征与短期临床获益的关系。","PFS":"无进展生存期，用于评价菌群特征对免疫治疗持续获益的预测价值。","OS":"总生存期，用于评价菌群特征与长期预后的关系。","irAE_incidence":"免疫相关不良事件发生率，用于评价菌群模式与毒性风险的关系。","grade_3_or_higher_irAE":"3级及以上免疫相关不良事件发生率，用于评估菌群特征对严重毒性的预测能力。","AUC":"预测模型受试者工作特征曲线下面积，用于评价疗效预测模型和irAE风险模型的判别能力。","C_index":"生存预测模型一致性指数，用于评价PFS和OS风险模型的预测性能。"},"ExpectedOutcome":"预期识别出一组与免疫治疗疗效相关的有益菌群特征及功能通路，并发现与免疫相关不良事件风险升高相关的菌群失衡模式。研究有望建立基于肠道菌群、外周免疫指标和临床变量的综合预测模型，实现晚期NSCLC免疫治疗患者的疗效分层和毒性风险预警，并为未来通过饮食、益生菌、抗生素管理或粪菌移植等方式优化免疫治疗结局提供临床依据。"}}

为确定研究空白，我先检索2型糖尿病中CGM相较传统指尖血糖监测对低血糖的既有证据，重点关注随机试验和系统综述。  
```action

[result]: 检索到的核心证据显示：在胰岛素治疗的2型糖尿病患者中，实时CGM或间歇扫描CGM可改善血糖可视化并增加治疗反馈；部分研究报告低血糖时间、夜间低血糖或低血糖暴露减少，但不同人群和干预强度下结果不一致。MOBILE等研究主要显示HbA1c改善，REPLACE等研究提示扫描式CGM可减少低血糖暴露；目前仍需要以“低血糖事件”为主要结局、纳入真实世界高风险T2D人群的前瞻性研究。  

基于检索结果，提出一个以低血糖事件为主要终点、兼顾患者报告结局和临床可实施性的研究方案。  
```action
{"tool":"propose","idea_plan":{"Idea":"研究题目：连续葡萄糖监测与传统指尖血糖监测对2型糖尿病患者低血糖事件影响的多中心随机对照研究。\n\n研究背景：2型糖尿病患者，尤其是接受胰岛素或磺脲类药物治疗者，存在显著低血糖风险。低血糖不仅可导致跌倒、心律失常、急诊就诊和住院，还会增加患者恐惧感，使其减少降糖治疗依从性，从而影响长期血糖控制。传统指尖血糖监测依赖患者在有限时间点主动测量，容易漏检夜间、无症状或短暂低血糖。连续葡萄糖监测能够提供全天候葡萄糖变化趋势、低血糖报警和时间低于目标范围等指标，理论上可更早发现并预防低血糖。然而，在2型糖尿病患者中，CGM相较传统指尖血糖监测对临床低血糖事件的实际影响仍需更明确证据，特别是在高低血糖风险患者和常规临床场景中。\n\n研究设计：本研究拟采用多中心、开放标签、平行分组随机对照设计，纳入成人2型糖尿病患者，重点包括正在使用基础胰岛素、预混胰岛素、多次胰岛素注射或磺脲类药物，且过去3个月有低血糖史或存在低血糖高风险特征者。受试者按1:1随机分配至CGM组或传统指尖血糖监测组，随访24周。CGM组使用实时或间歇扫描CGM，并接受标准化低血糖报警设置、趋势箭头解读和用药/饮食/运动调整教育；对照组继续进行常规指尖血糖监测，并接受同等频率的糖尿病教育和随访。研究将尽量保持两组临床接触频率一致，以减少教育强度差异导致的偏倚。\n\n主要结局：主要结局为随访期间经确认的低血糖事件发生率，定义为血糖<70 mg/dL或3.9 mmol/L，或需要他人协助的严重低血糖事件。关键次要结局包括：血糖<54 mg/dL或3.0 mmol/L的临床显著低血糖事件发生率；夜间低血糖事件；CGM组中的时间低于范围、时间在范围内和血糖变异性；HbA1c变化；急诊或住院相关低血糖事件；糖尿病治疗满意度、低血糖恐惧量表评分和生活质量。\n\n潜在临床意义：若CGM能够显著减少低血糖事件，研究将支持在高风险2型糖尿病患者中更早、更广泛地使用CGM，并为医保支付、指南推荐和门诊管理路径提供证据。即使HbA1c改善有限，若低血糖负担、夜间低血糖和患者恐惧感下降，也可能改善治疗安全性、依从性和生活质量。本研究还可帮助识别最可能从CGM获益的人群，例如胰岛素使用者、既往低血糖者、老年患者或低血糖感知受损者。","ImplementationSteps":{"1":"研究准备与伦理审批：制定多中心统一方案，明确低血糖定义、报警阈值、指尖血糖校验要求、随访窗口和安全处理流程；完成伦理审批、研究者培训和数据采集系统建设。","2":"受试者招募与随机分组：纳入18岁及以上2型糖尿病患者，优先选择使用胰岛素或磺脲类且有低血糖风险者；排除近期严重疾病、无法使用设备或妊娠者；按中心、治疗方案和既往低血糖史进行分层随机。","3":"干预实施：CGM组佩戴连续葡萄糖监测设备并接受标准化教育，包括报警处理、趋势箭头解释和低血糖预防；对照组按指南进行传统指尖血糖监测，并接受同等频率教育。两组均由临床医生依据预设原则调整治疗。","4":"随访与数据收集：在基线、第4、12和24周收集HbA1c、低血糖事件、用药变化、急诊住院情况和患者报告结局；CGM组自动导出葡萄糖指标，对照组记录指尖血糖和症状性低血糖日志。","5":"统计分析：采用意向性治疗分析，比较两组低血糖事件发生率，可使用负二项回归或Poisson回归并校正暴露时间；对夜间低血糖、严重低血糖、HbA1c、生活质量和低血糖恐惧进行预设次要分析与亚组分析。"},"ImplementationOrder":["1-2"],"Dataset":"前瞻性多中心随机对照试验数据集。目标样本为约300-500例成人2型糖尿病患者，随访24周。数据包括人口学特征、糖尿病病程、用药方案、既往低血糖史、HbA1c、肾功能、CGM葡萄糖曲线、指尖血糖记录、低血糖事件日志、急诊/住院记录和患者报告结局量表。","EvaluationMetrics":{"主要结局":"24周内经确认低血糖事件发生率，即血糖<70 mmol/L事件发生率，用于评估更严重低血糖暴露。","严重低血糖":"需要他人协助、急救处理、急诊就诊或住院的低血糖事件比例。","夜间低血糖":"夜间时段，例如00:00-06:00，发生的低血糖事件数及持续时间。","血糖控制":"HbA1c从基线至24周的变化，以及CGM组的时间在范围内、时间低于范围和血糖变异系数。","患者报告结局":"低血糖恐惧量表、糖尿病治疗满意度和生活质量评分变化。","安全性和可接受性":"设备相关不良事件、退出率、CGM佩戴依从性和监测数据完整性。"},"ExpectedOutcome":"预期CGM组较传统指尖血糖监测组可降低总体低血糖事件率，尤其减少夜间和无症状低血糖，并可能提高时间在范围内、降低血糖变异性和低血糖恐惧。在不增加严重不良事件的前提下，研究结果有望证明CGM在高风险2型糖尿病患者中的临床价值，为个体化血糖监测策略、临床指南更新和医保覆盖决策提供依据。"}}

topic: [v15/en/idea_plan/clinical_medicine] Investigate whether early point-of-care ultrasound in the emergency department improves time to diagnosis and clinical outcomes in patients presenting with undi
full_prompt: Investigate whether early point-of-care ultrasound in the emergency department improves time to diagnosis and clinical outcomes in patients presenting with undifferentiated dyspnea.
completion:
Early ED point-of-care ultrasound (POCUS) for undifferentiated dyspnea is clinically plausible because lung/cardiac/IVC ultrasound can rapidly distinguish pulmonary edema, COPD/asthma, pneumonia, pneumothorax, and shock-related causes. I’ll first look for existing evidence and gaps around diagnostic speed and outcomes.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"emergency department point-of-care ultrasound undifferentiated dyspnea time to diagnosis clinical outcomes randomized trial lung ultrasound cardiac ultrasound"}
```

[result]: Simulated search result: Existing studies suggest ED POCUS improves diagnostic accuracy and may shorten time to correct diagnosis in acute dyspnea, especially when lung ultrasound is combined with focused cardiac ultrasound. However, evidence is heterogeneous; many studies are observational, focus on diagnostic accuracy rather than patient-centered outcomes, and often do not measure downstream endpoints such as time to treatment, admission disposition, ICU transfer, length of stay, or mortality.

A key gap appears to be whether protocolized early multi-organ POCUS changes clinically meaningful outcomes beyond diagnostic accuracy. I’ll read a representative evidence summary to refine the proposed study design.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"systematic review POCUS undifferentiated dyspnea emergency department outcomes time to diagnosis"}
```

[result]: Simulated read result: Review-level evidence indicates lung ultrasound has high accuracy for acute heart failure and pneumothorax, and combined cardiopulmonary ultrasound improves differentiation of dyspnea etiologies. Most included studies report improved diagnostic confidence and shorter diagnostic workup, but few are randomized or powered for clinical outcomes. Common limitations include variable operator skill, inconsistent ultrasound protocols, lack of blinding, and inconsistent timing of POCUS relative to standard testing.

```action
{"tool":"propose","idea_plan":{"Idea":"Conduct a pragmatic randomized controlled trial testing whether early protocolized ED cardiopulmonary POCUS improves time to diagnosis and clinical outcomes in adults presenting with undifferentiated dyspnea.","ImplementationSteps":{"1":"Enroll adult ED patients presenting with acute undifferentiated dyspnea before a definitive diagnosis is established; exclude patients requiring immediate intubation or those with an obvious traumatic/surgical cause.","2":"Randomize patients to early POCUS plus usual care versus usual care alone. The POCUS protocol should be completed within 15 minutes of clinician assessment and include lung ultrasound, focused cardiac ultrasound, and IVC assessment.","3":"Record time from ED arrival and from clinician assessment to leading working diagnosis, first targeted therapy, and final adjudicated diagnosis. Use blinded expert adjudication of final diagnosis based on the full clinical record.","4":"Collect clinical outcomes including ED length of stay, hospital admission, ICU admission, need for noninvasive or invasive ventilation, in-hospital mortality, 30-day revisit/readmission, and diagnostic error or major change in diagnosis.","5":"Stratify or adjust analyses by operator experience, suspected disease category, baseline severity, and availability/timing of chest radiography, BNP, troponin, CT, and formal echocardiography."},"ImplementationOrder":["1-2","3","4","5"],"Dataset":"Prospective multicenter ED trial dataset of adults with acute undifferentiated dyspnea, including timestamped diagnostic and treatment events, POCUS findings, usual diagnostic testing, adjudicated final diagnosis, and short-term clinical outcomes through 30 days.","EvaluationMetrics":{"Primary metric":"Median time from ED arrival to correct adjudicated diagnosis.","Secondary diagnostic metrics":"Time to first targeted therapy; diagnostic accuracy of initial ED diagnosis; rate of major diagnostic revision after initial assessment.","Clinical outcome metrics":"ED length of stay, hospital length of stay, ICU admission, need for ventilation, in-hospital mortality, 30-day ED revisit or readmission.","Process metrics":"Time to POCUS completion, proportion of adequate scans, downstream imaging use, and clinician diagnostic confidence."},"ExpectedOutcome":"Early protocolized cardiopulmonary POCUS is expected to reduce time to correct diagnosis and time to targeted therapy, particularly for acute heart failure, pneumothorax, pleural effusion, and pneumonia. The effect on mortality may be modest, but improvements are expected in ED efficiency, diagnostic confidence, and potentially reduced unnecessary imaging or delayed treatment."}}
```

topic: [v15/en/idea_plan/clinical_medicine] Write a free-text research proposal evaluating whether pharmacist-led medication reconciliation reduces 30-day hospital readmissions among older adults discharg
full_prompt: Write a free-text research proposal evaluating whether pharmacist-led medication reconciliation reduces 30-day hospital readmissions among older adults discharged from internal medicine wards.
completion:
Rationale: I’ll first identify the existing evidence base to position the proposal and justify the study design. Then I’ll synthesize it into a focused clinical medicine research plan.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"pharmacist-led medication reconciliation 30-day readmission older adults internal medicine discharge randomized trial"}
```

[result]: Prior studies and systematic reviews suggest pharmacist-led medication reconciliation can reduce medication discrepancies after discharge, but effects on 30-day readmissions are mixed. Benefits appear more likely in older adults, patients with polypharmacy, and interventions that combine reconciliation with discharge counseling and post-discharge follow-up.

Rationale: Because the evidence is heterogeneous, the proposal should use a pragmatic randomized design with robust outcome ascertainment and clinically relevant secondary endpoints.

```action
{"tool":"propose","idea_plan":{"Idea":"Evaluate whether a pharmacist-led medication reconciliation and discharge medication counseling intervention reduces 30-day all-cause hospital readmissions among adults aged 65 years and older discharged from internal medicine wards. The study will address an important care-transition problem: older adults frequently leave hospital with medication changes, polypharmacy, and incomplete understanding of their regimen, all of which may contribute to adverse drug events and preventable readmissions. We propose a pragmatic, parallel-group randomized controlled trial comparing usual discharge care with a structured pharmacist-led reconciliation program delivered before discharge, with optional early post-discharge telephone follow-up.","ImplementationSteps":{"1":"Design a pragmatic randomized controlled trial enrolling hospitalized adults aged ≥65 years discharged from internal medicine wards to home or assisted living. Exclude patients discharged to hospice, transferred to another acute facility, or unable to provide consent without a caregiver/proxy.","2":"Randomize participants 1:1 to usual care or pharmacist-led medication reconciliation. Usual care will include standard physician/nursing discharge processes. Escalate clinically significant problems to the primary care clinician or hospital team.","5":"Collect baseline covariates including age, sex, comorbidity burden, number of medications, prior admissions, cognition or delirium status where available, renal function, high-risk medication use, living situation, and health literacy proxy measures.","6":"Ascertain outcomes using hospital administrative data, electronic health records, patient/caregiver telephone follow-up, and regional readmission databases when available. Analyze by intention-to-treat, using logistic regression or time-to-event models adjusted for prespecified baseline risk factors.","7":"Perform subgroup analyses among patients with polypharmacy, prior hospitalization, heart failure/COPD/diabetes, impaired renal function, and high-risk medication exposure. Conduct a process evaluation measuring intervention fidelity, number and type of discrepancies identified, pharmacist time, and clinician acceptance of recommendations."},"ImplementationOrder":["1","2","3","4","5","6","7"],"Dataset":"Prospective trial dataset of adults aged ≥65 years discharged from internal medicine wards, linked to electronic health record medication data, pharmacy reconciliation forms, discharge summaries, administrative readmission records, and 30-day patient/caregiver follow-up calls.","EvaluationMetrics":{"Primary metric":"30-day all-cause hospital readmission after index discharge, measured as the proportion of participants readmitted within 30 days.","Secondary clinical metrics":"30-day potentially medication-related readmissions, emergency department visits, adverse drug events, mortality, and unplanned primary care or urgent care visits.","Medication safety metrics":"Number of clinically significant medication discrepancies at discharge and at 3–7 days post-discharge; number of pharmacist interventions accepted by physicians; number of high-risk medication issues resolved.","Patient-centered metrics":"Patient or caregiver understanding of medication regimen, medication adherence at 30 days, satisfaction with discharge medication counseling, and ability to obtain prescribed medications.","Health system metrics":"Length of index hospitalization, pharmacist time per patient, cost per readmission prevented, and net cost difference between intervention and usual care."},"ExpectedOutcome":"The pharmacist-led intervention is expected to reduce clinically significant medication discrepancies and may reduce 30-day readmissions, particularly among older adults with polypharmacy or high baseline readmission risk. Even if the effect on all-cause readmission is modest, the study should clarify whether improved medication safety, fewer adverse drug events, and better patient understanding justify routine pharmacist involvement in internal medicine discharge transitions."}}

topic: [v15/en/idea_plan/clinical_medicine] Prepare a free-text research proposal assessing the safety and effectiveness of de-escalating antibiotic therapy based on rapid molecular diagnostics in adults
full_prompt: Prepare a free-text research proposal assessing the safety and effectiveness of de-escalating antibiotic therapy based on rapid molecular diagnostics in adults with suspected sepsis.
completion:
I’ll first search for current evidence on rapid molecular diagnostics and antibiotic de-escalation in adult sepsis, then synthesize a feasible clinical research proposal focused on safety and effectiveness outcomes.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"adult sepsis rapid molecular diagnostics antibiotic de-escalation safety effectiveness trial bloodstream infection stewardship"}
```

[result]: Rapid molecular diagnostic tests for suspected sepsis/bloodstream infection include multiplex PCR blood culture panels, direct-from-blood pathogen detection, and resistance gene assays. Evidence suggests faster organism identification and resistance detection can reduce time to appropriate therapy and support antimicrobial stewardship, particularly when paired with stewardship review. Key safety concerns include missed pathogens, false positives/negatives, polymicrobial infection, contamination, and premature narrowing of therapy. Common outcomes include mortality, time to de-escalation, antibiotic days of therapy, ICU/hospital length of stay, Clostridioides difficile infection, acute kidney injury, recurrent infection, and antimicrobial resistance.

```action

Exclude patients with profound immunosuppression, expected death within 24 hours, or clear noninfectious syndromes.","2":"Randomize patients or hospital units to either rapid molecular diagnostic testing plus real-time antimicrobial stewardship review or usual care using conventional culture, susceptibility testing, and routine stewardship processes.","3":"In the intervention arm, perform rapid molecular testing on positive blood cultures or direct-from-blood specimens, including pathogen identification and selected resistance markers. Stewardship clinicians issue standardized recommendations for de-escalation, continuation, escalation, or discontinuation based on test results, clinical status, infection source, and local guidelines.","4":"Collect baseline severity data, infection source, empiric antibiotic regimen, molecular and culture results, stewardship recommendations, physician acceptance, antibiotic changes, adverse events, and clinical outcomes through 30 days.","5":"Compare effectiveness outcomes such as time to antibiotic de-escalation and total broad-spectrum antibiotic exposure, while testing safety through mortality, clinical deterioration, recurrent infection, and need for antibiotic re-escalation.","6":"Perform subgroup analyses by ICU status, pathogen type, gram-positive versus gram-negative infection, culture-positive versus culture-negative sepsis, and presence of resistance genes."},"ImplementationOrder":["1","2","3","4","5","6"],"Dataset":"Prospectively collected multicenter adult sepsis cohort including electronic health record data, microbiology results, rapid molecular diagnostic outputs, antimicrobial administration records, stewardship recommendations, and 30-day clinical outcomes. 基于检索结果，将研究设计为基层医院多中心前瞻性诊断效能评估，重点比较“常规阅片”与“AI辅助阅片”的敏感度、特异度、阳性预测值、阴性预测值和报告效率。  
```action
{"tool":"propose","idea_plan":{"Idea":"研究题目：人工智能辅助胸部CT筛查早期肺癌在基层医院中的诊断效能评估。\n\n研究背景与目的：肺癌早期症状隐匿，基层医院承担大量初筛任务，但影像科医师经验、工作负荷和肺小结节识别能力存在差异。人工智能辅助胸部CT分析系统可自动识别、标注和量化肺结节，理论上有助于提升早期肺癌筛查质量。本研究拟在基层医院真实诊疗环境中评价AI辅助胸部低剂量CT筛查早期肺癌的诊断效能、报告效率和临床可用性。\n\n研究假设：与基层医院常规人工阅片相比，AI辅助阅片能够提高早期肺癌，尤其是I期肺癌或需进一步诊治的高危肺结节的检出敏感度，并缩短影像报告时间；同时其特异度和假阳性率处于临床可接受范围，不显著增加不必要的有创检查或转诊负担。","ImplementationSteps":{"1":"研究设计：采用多中心、前瞻性、配对诊断试验设计，在若干基层医院连续纳入接受胸部低剂量CT肺癌筛查的受试者。每例CT影像分别进行两种判读：基层影像医师常规阅片，以及AI辅助下的医师阅片。两种判读结果记录肺结节数量、位置、最大径、密度类型、恶性风险分层、是否建议随访或转诊。最终诊断参考标准由上级医院胸部影像专家复核、病理结果、临床诊断及至少12个月影像随访综合确定。","2":"研究对象：纳入标准包括：年龄一般为40岁及以上；接受基层医院胸部低剂量CT肺癌筛查；具备完整DICOM影像资料和基本临床信息；愿意签署知情同意；可完成随访。可优先纳入肺癌高危人群，如长期吸烟、二手烟暴露、职业粉尘或石棉暴露、既往恶性肿瘤史、慢性阻塞性肺疾病或肺癌家族史者。排除标准包括：既往已确诊肺癌或正在接受肺癌治疗；近期已知肺部感染、活动性肺结核或弥漫性肺间质病变严重影响结节判断；胸部CT图像质量明显不合格；妊娠期女性；无法提供知情同意或无法完成随访者；资料严重缺失者。","3":"主要观察指标：主要终点为早期肺癌检出敏感度和特异度。次要终点包括阳性预测值、阴性预测值、受试者工作特征曲线下面积、假阳性率、假阴性率、结节级别检出率、I期肺癌检出比例、影像报告时间、转诊率、进一步检查率以及不同经验医师使用AI前后的诊断一致性。","4":"统计分析思路：以受试者和病灶两个层面分别分析诊断效能。计数资料以例数和百分比描述，连续资料以均数±标准差或中位数和四分位数描述。采用配对设计比较常规阅片与AI辅助阅片的敏感度、特异度、阳性预测值和阴性预测值，可使用McNemar检验或广义估计方程处理同一患者多病灶相关性。构建ROC曲线并比较AUC差异。报告时间采用配对t检验或Wilcoxon符号秩检验。进一步采用多因素Logistic回归分析AI辅助诊断准确性的影响因素，如年龄、吸烟史、结节大小、结节类型、医院层级和医师年资。可进行亚组分析，包括纯磨玻璃结节、部分实性结节、小于6 mm结节、不同医师经验组和不同基层医院。样本量估算基于预期敏感度提升幅度，例如假设AI辅助将敏感度由75%提高至88%，按双侧α=0.05、效能80%估算，并考虑失访率。","5":"伦理考虑：研究应经各参与医院伦理委员会批准，并在研究开始前完成注册。所有受试者签署书面知情同意，明确说明AI仅作为辅助工具，最终诊疗决策由具有资质的临床医师完成。研究使用低剂量CT方案，遵循辐射防护最优化原则，避免因研究额外增加不必要辐射暴露。影像和临床数据在分析前进行去标识化处理，限定授权人员访问，符合个人信息保护和医学数据安全要求。对AI提示的高危结果建立明确复核和转诊流程，避免漏诊；同时对假阳性风险进行充分告知，减少不必要焦虑和过度诊疗。研究过程中设立数据安全和质量控制机制，定期监测误诊、漏诊、随访缺失和不良事件。"},"ImplementationOrder":["1-2"],"Dataset":"前瞻性收集基层医院胸部低剂量CT筛查数据，包括DICOM影像、人口学资料、吸烟和职业暴露史、既往病史、AI输出结果、医师阅片结果、转诊及进一步检查记录、病理结果或12个月以上影像随访结局。","EvaluationMetrics":{"sensitivity":"早期肺癌或临床需处理高危肺结节的检出敏感度，作为主要诊断效能指标。","specificity":"非肺癌或低风险结节被正确排除的比例，用于评估AI辅助是否造成过多假阳性。","AUC":"基于恶性风险评分绘制ROC曲线并计算曲线下面积，综合评价区分能力。","PPV_NPV":"阳性预测值和阴性预测值，用于评价基层筛查场景下AI结果的临床解释价值。","false_positive_rate":"假阳性率，用于衡量不必要随访、转诊或检查的潜在负担。","reporting_time":"从影像导入至报告完成的时间，用于评价AI对基层工作效率的影响。","agreement":"AI辅助前后医师诊断一致性及专家复核一致性，可用Kappa值描述。"},"ExpectedOutcome":"预期AI辅助胸部CT筛查可提高基层医院早期肺癌和高危肺结节检出敏感度，缩短报告时间，并改善低年资医师与专家判断的一致性；但可能伴随一定假阳性增加。研究将明确AI在基层肺癌筛查中的真实诊断效能、适用边界和质量控制要求，为建立基层筛查—上级医院复核—规范随访转诊的肺癌早诊路径提供依据。"}}
```

topic: [v15/en/idea_plan/clinical_medicine] Design a prospective cohort study to identify clinical and biomarker predictors of progression from prediabetes to type 2 diabetes in primary care populations.
full_prompt: Design a prospective cohort study to identify clinical and biomarker predictors of progression from prediabetes to type 2 diabetes in primary care populations.
completion:
I’ll first verify current diagnostic criteria and commonly reported predictors so the cohort design aligns with clinical practice and uses actionable biomarkers.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"prediabetes progression to type 2 diabetes predictors HbA1c fasting glucose BMI primary care biomarkers cohort study"}
```

[result]: Simulated results found that progression from prediabetes to type 2 diabetes is commonly predicted by baseline HbA1c, fasting plasma glucose, BMI/waist circumference, age, family history, ethnicity, blood pressure, triglycerides/HDL, liver enzymes, insulin resistance indices, and inflammatory markers such as hs-CRP. ADA diagnostic thresholds define prediabetes as HbA1c 5.7–6.4%, fasting plasma glucose 100–125 mg/dL, or 2-hour OGTT glucose 140–199 mg/dL; diabetes is commonly defined as HbA1c ≥6.5%, fasting glucose ≥126 mg/dL, 2-hour glucose ≥200 mg/dL, or clinical diagnosis/medication initiation.

I’ll read a guideline-style source to anchor eligibility and outcome definitions.

```action
{"tool":"read","source":"ADA Standards of Care diagnostic criteria for diabetes and prediabetes"}
```

[result]: Simulated guideline read confirms diagnostic categories: prediabetes includes HbA1c 5.7–6.4%, impaired fasting glucose 100–125 mg/dL, or impaired glucose tolerance with 2-hour plasma glucose 140–199 mg/dL. Type 2 diabetes diagnosis can be based on HbA1c ≥6.5%, fasting plasma glucose ≥126 mg/dL, 2-hour OGTT glucose ≥200 mg/dL, random glucose ≥200 mg/dL with symptoms, or initiation of glucose-lowering medication after clinician diagnosis.

topic: [v15/zh/idea_plan/clinical_medicine] 请撰写关于围手术期营养干预对胃癌根治术患者术后并发症和恢复质量影响的自由文本研究提案。内容应包括临床问题、研究方法、样本量估计思路和预期成果。
full_prompt: 请撰写关于围手术期营养干预对胃癌根治术患者术后并发症和恢复质量影响的自由文本研究提案。内容应包括临床问题、研究方法、样本量估计思路和预期成果。
completion:
围手术期营养干预在胃癌根治术中的价值通常与 ERAS、营养风险筛查和免疫营养相关，因此先检索近期指南与临床研究共识，以明确研究设计和主要终点。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"perioperative nutritional intervention gastric cancer radical gastrectomy postoperative complications recovery quality randomized trial ERAS ESPEN guideline"}
```
[result]: 检索到的核心信息显示：胃癌患者术前营养不良或营养风险发生率较高，NRS-2002、PG-SGA、白蛋白/前白蛋白、体重下降常用于评估；ESPEN 与 ERAS 相关指南建议对存在营养风险的患者进行术前7–14天营养支持，必要时联合口服营养补充或肠内营养；免疫营养可能降低感染性并发症、缩短住院时间，但对总体并发症、生活质量和术后恢复质量的证据仍存在异质性。常用结局包括Clavien-Dindo分级并发症、感染性并发症、术后首次排气/进食时间、住院天数、再入院率、QoR-15或EORTC QLQ-C30/STO22等。

基于上述证据缺口，拟提出一项前瞻性随机对照研究，重点评价“标准化围手术期营养干预”对并发症与恢复质量的综合影响。  
```action
{"tool":"propose","idea_plan":{"Idea":"临床问题：胃癌根治术患者常伴有肿瘤相关消耗、进食减少、贫血和低蛋白状态，围手术期营养风险可能增加吻合口漏、肺部感染、切口感染、胃排空延迟等术后并发症，并影响术后体力恢复和生活质量。当前临床虽普遍重视ERAS管理，但营养干预的启动时机、持续时间、干预强度及其对患者报告结局的影响仍需进一步验证。本研究拟评估以营养风险筛查为基础的标准化围手术期营养干预，对胃癌根治术患者术后30天并发症和术后恢复质量的影响。研究假设为：与常规围手术期管理相比，个体化营养评估、术前7–14天营养优化、术后早期肠内营养及出院后短期营养随访可降低术后并发症发生率，并改善患者术后恢复质量和短期生活质量。","ImplementationSteps":{"1":"研究设计：开展单中心或多中心、前瞻性、随机、对照、开放标签临床研究。纳入拟行胃癌根治术的成年患者，包括远端胃切除、全胃切除或近端胃切除患者。排除标准包括急诊手术、严重肝肾功能衰竭、无法经胃肠道摄入或随访困难者。入组后使用NRS-2002和PG-SGA进行营养风险评估，并收集BMI、近3–6个月体重下降比例、白蛋白、前白蛋白、血红蛋白、CRP等基线指标。","2":"干预措施：对照组接受常规围手术期处理和一般饮食宣教。干预组接受标准化围手术期营养路径：术前进行营养师评估，依据能量25–30 kcal/kg/d、蛋白质1.2–1.5 g/kg/d制定方案；营养风险患者术前给予7–14天口服营养补充或肠内营养，必要时联合免疫营养制剂；术后遵循ERAS原则尽早恢复肠内摄入，根据耐受情况阶梯式增加能量和蛋白摄入；出院后继续2–4周营养随访与饮食调整。","3":"主要终点：术后30天总体并发症发生率，按Clavien-Dindo分级记录，并重点分析Ⅱ级及以上并发症。次要终点包括感染性并发症、吻合口漏、切口感染、肺部感染、胃排空延迟、首次排气时间、首次经口进食时间、术后住院天数、30天再入院率、术后体重下降、白蛋白/前白蛋白变化、QoR-15评分、EORTC QLQ-C30或STO22生活质量评分。","4":"样本量估计思路：以术后30天总体并发症发生率作为主要样本量估计依据。参考既往胃癌根治术研究，常规管理组并发症率可假设约为35%；若标准化营养干预可将并发症率降至20%，采用双侧α=0.05、检验效能80%，按两独立比例比较估算，每组约需135–145例。考虑约10%失访、方案违背或手术方式改变，计划总样本量约300–320例。若以QoR-15作为关键次要终点，可设定最小临床重要差异约8分、标准差约18分进行连续变量样本量验证，以确保研究对恢复质量差异亦有足够把握。","5":"统计分析：采用意向性分析为主，符合方案分析为辅。分类变量使用χ²检验或Fisher精确检验，连续变量根据分布采用t检验或Mann-Whitney U检验。主要终点可进一步构建多因素logistic回归模型，校正年龄、性别、TNM分期、手术方式、营养风险状态、糖尿病、术前新辅助治疗等混杂因素。对存在营养风险与无营养风险患者进行预设亚组分析，以探索干预获益人群。"},"ImplementationOrder":["1-2","3-4","5"],"Dataset":"研究对象为拟接受胃癌根治术的住院患者。数据来源包括电子病历、围手术期麻醉和手术记录、营养评估表、实验室检查、术后并发症登记表、出院随访记录及患者报告结局量表。关键变量包括人口学资料、肿瘤分期、手术方式、营养风险评分、营养摄入达标率、术后并发症、住院时间、再入院情况和生活质量评分。","EvaluationMetrics":{"primary_metric":"术后30天总体并发症发生率，按Clavien-Dindo分级判定，比较干预组与对照组差异。","secondary_metric_1":"术后30天Ⅱ级及以上并发症、感染性并发症、吻合口漏、肺部感染和切口感染发生率。","secondary_metric_2":"恢复过程指标，包括首次排气时间、首次经口进食时间、术后住院天数和30天再入院率。","secondary_metric_3":"营养与炎症相关指标，包括体重变化、白蛋白、前白蛋白、血红蛋白、CRP及营养摄入达标率。","secondary_metric_4":"患者报告结局，包括术后第3天、第7天或出院时QoR-15评分，以及术后1个月EORTC QLQ-C30/STO22生活质量评分。"},"ExpectedOutcome":"预期标准化围手术期营养干预可降低胃癌根治术患者术后30天并发症，尤其是感染性并发症和中重度并发症；同时可促进胃肠功能恢复，缩短住院时间，减少术后体重和蛋白水平下降，并改善患者早期恢复质量和短期生活质量。研究结果有望为胃癌根治术患者建立可操作的营养管理路径，明确营养风险筛查、术前营养优化、术后早期肠内营养和出院后随访在ERAS体系中的具体价值，为临床制定个体化围手术期营养策略提供证据。"}}

topic: [v15/en/idea_plan/clinical_medicine] Explore associations between social determinants of health, medication adherence, and blood pressure control among patients with resistant hypertension in urban
full_prompt: Explore associations between social determinants of health, medication adherence, and blood pressure control among patients with resistant hypertension in urban outpatient clinics.
completion:
Resistant hypertension is strongly influenced by medication adherence and structural barriers common in urban settings, so I’ll first scan for evidence on social determinants, adherence, and BP outcomes in this population.

```action

[result]: Studies suggest that low income, medication cost, limited health literacy, unstable housing, transportation barriers, food insecurity, and neighborhood deprivation are associated with poorer antihypertensive adherence and lower rates of blood pressure control. In resistant hypertension, nonadherence and social barriers are common contributors to apparent treatment resistance, especially in safety-net and urban clinic populations.

```action

```

[result]: Key methodological recommendations include distinguishing true resistant hypertension from pseudo-resistance, measuring adherence using pharmacy refill data and/or validated self-report, capturing multidimensional SDOH at patient and neighborhood levels, and using multivariable or mediation models to test whether adherence partially explains the relationship between SDOH and BP control.

topic: [v15/zh/idea_plan/clinical_medicine] 设计一项临床研究，评估老年髋部骨折患者术后早期综合康复路径对功能恢复、谵妄发生率和住院时间的影响。请给出研究框架、干预内容、评估工具和数据分析计划。

completion:
先检索老年髋部骨折术后康复、谵妄预防与住院结局相关证据，以便把研究设计建立在现有临床路径和评估工具基础上。

基于检索结果，拟设计一项前瞻性随机对照临床研究，比较“术后早期综合康复路径”与常规护理对老年髋部骨折患者功能恢复、谵妄和住院时间的影响。  
```action
{
  "tool": "propose",

    "Idea": "开展一项单中心或多中心、前瞻性、平行分组随机对照临床研究，评估老年髋部骨折患者术后早期综合康复路径对术后功能恢复、谵妄发生率和住院时间的影响。研究对象为年龄≥65岁、因低能量损伤导致髋部骨折并接受手术治疗的患者。干预组接受结构化早期综合康复路径，对照组接受常规术后康复与护理。",
    "ImplementationSteps": {
      "1": "研究设计：采用随机对照试验设计，按1:1比例分配至干预组和对照组。随机化可采用计算机生成随机序列，并按年龄、骨折类型或术前认知状态进行分层。评估人员尽量实施盲法。",
      "2": "研究对象：纳入年龄≥65岁、股骨颈骨折或转子间骨折、接受内固定或关节置换术、术后生命体征稳定且可参与康复评估者。排除术前长期卧床、严重痴呆无法配合评估、终末期疾病、严重多发伤或术后入住ICU时间过长者。",
      "3": "干预内容：干预组在术后24至48小时内启动综合康复路径，包括早期床边坐起和下床活动、个体化步行训练、肌力和平衡训练、疼痛规范化管理、营养评估与蛋白补充、贫血和电解质管理、睡眠管理、视听辅助、导尿管和约束最小化、谵妄风险筛查、家属参与、出院计划和居家康复指导。由骨科医生、老年医学科医生、康复治疗师、护士、营养师和药师共同执行。",
      "4": "对照处理：对照组接受医院常规术后治疗、护理和康复宣教，不使用统一的早期综合康复路径，但不限制必要的临床治疗。",
      "5": "评估时间点：基线评估在术前或术后24小时内完成；术后每日评估谵妄直至出院；功能评估在术后第3天、第7天或出院时、术后30天和术后90天进行；同时记录住院时间、并发症、再入院率和出院去向。",
      "6": "主要结局：功能恢复水平，可用Barthel Index或Functional Independence Measure在术后30天或90天的变化量表示。关键次要结局包括住院期间谵妄发生率和急性住院时间。",
      "7": "评估工具：功能状态采用Barthel Index、Functional Independence Measure、Timed Up and Go或Short Physical Performance Battery；谵妄采用Confusion Assessment Method或4AT每日筛查；疼痛采用Numeric Rating Scale；认知基线采用Mini-Cog、MMSE或MoCA；衰弱采用Clinical Frailty Scale；营养状态采用Mini Nutritional Assessment-Short Form；生活质量可采用EQ-5D-5L。",
      "8": "样本量计划：根据主要结局Barthel Index改善差异进行估算。例如假设干预组较对照组30天Barthel Index平均提高8至10分，标准差约20分，双侧α=0.05，检验效能80%，并考虑10%至15%失访率，估算每组约80至120例。最终样本量需根据预实验或既往数据精确计算。",
      "9": "数据分析计划：采用意向性分析为主，符合方案分析为辅。连续变量如功能评分和住院时间，若近似正态分布采用t检验或线性回归；非正态分布采用Mann-Whitney U检验或广义线性模型。二分类结局如谵妄发生率采用卡方检验、Logistic回归，并调整年龄、性别、基线认知功能、骨折类型、手术方式、ASA分级和合并症指数等混杂因素。",

      "12": "伦理与安全：研究需获得伦理委员会批准并取得患者或法定代理人知情同意。康复过程中监测跌倒、伤口并发症、疼痛加重、心肺不适等不良事件，并设置暂停康复标准。"
    },

    ],

    "EvaluationMetrics": {

    "ExpectedOutcome": "预计术后早期综合康复路径可显著改善老年髋部骨折患者术后30天和90天功能恢复，降低住院期间谵妄发生率，缩短住院时间，并可能减少部分术后并发症和提高回家出院比例。研究结果可为建立标准化老年髋部骨折围手术期康复管理路径提供临床证据。"
  }
}

topic: [v15/zh/idea_plan/clinical_medicine] 针对急性缺血性卒中患者机械取栓后早期血压管理策略，构思一项多中心随机对照试验。请说明干预窗口、血压目标、主要终点、次要终点及安全性监测。
full_prompt: 针对急性缺血性卒中患者机械取栓后早期血压管理策略，构思一项多中心随机对照试验。请说明干预窗口、血压目标、主要终点、次要终点及安全性监测。
completion:
我先快速检索既往机械取栓后血压管理试验，以避免提出已被证伪或存在安全隐患的过低血压目标。重点关注“强化降压是否有害”以及指南通常阈值。  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"acute ischemic stroke mechanical thrombectomy post EVT blood pressure randomized trial BP-TARGET ENCHANTED2/MT OPTIMAL-BP systolic target"}
```  
[result]: 检索摘要显示：BP-TARGET比较100–129 vs 130–185 mmHg未显示减少出血转化；ENCHANTED2/MT中<120 mmHg强化降压较140–180 mmHg预后更差；OPTIMAL-BP中<140 mmHg策略在成功再通患者中因功能结局不佳提前终止。指南多建议取栓后24小时血压<180/105 mmHg，但最佳下限与中等目标仍不确定。
基于上述证据，试验设计应避免“过度强化降压”，转向检验一个更温和、设定下限的目标区间，以减少高灌注相关出血同时避免低灌注。下面给出多中心RCT方案草案。  
```action
{"tool":"propose","idea_plan":{"Idea":"开展一项前瞻性、多中心、开放标签、盲法终点评估的随机对照试验，比较机械取栓成功再通后早期“温和目标血压管理”与“标准指南式血压管理”对急性缺血性卒中患者90天功能结局的影响。核心假设是：在避免收缩压过低的前提下，将收缩压维持在140–160 mmHg可优于常规<180/105 mmHg策略，降低出血转化和再灌注损伤，同时不增加低灌注性神经恶化。","ImplementationSteps":{"1":"研究对象：纳入18岁及以上急性前循环或后循环大血管闭塞卒中、接受机械取栓且达到成功再通eTICI/mTICI 2b–3的患者；随机化需在再通后60分钟内完成。排除基线严重低血压、需升压药维持、持续颅内出血、预期寿命<90天或需要严格特殊血压目标的患者。","2":"干预窗口：随机化后立即启动干预，持续24小时；第24–48小时进入过渡期，建议两组均按当地指南平稳降压并避免血压剧烈波动。所有患者接受连续或高频无创/有创血压监测。","3":"干预组血压目标：收缩压140–160 mmHg，明确安全下限为不低于130 mmHg；若SBP>160，优先使用可滴定静脉降压药如尼卡地平、乌拉地尔或拉贝洛尔；若SBP<130或出现神经功能恶化，暂停降压、补液或按方案升压。","4":"对照组血压目标：标准指南式管理，目标SBP<180 mmHg且DBP<105 mmHg，建议维持SBP 160–180 mmHg区间，避免SBP<130；仅当SBP≥180或DBP≥105时启动或强化降压。","5":"随机化与分层：1:1随机，按中心、再通程度2b vs 2c/3、是否静脉溶栓、闭塞部位前循环 vs 后循环、基线ASPECTS或梗死核心体积分层。终点评估者和影像判读者盲法。","6":"主要终点：90天改良Rankin量表mRS的全范围有序分布转移分析，作为主要有效性终点；关键补充分析为90天功能独立率mRS 0–2。","7":"次要终点：24–36小时症状性颅内出血率；任何颅内出血和PH2型出血；24小时NIHSS变化及早期神经功能恶化；24–72小时梗死体积增长；7天或出院时NIHSS；90天死亡率、mRS 0–1、mRS 0–3、EQ-5D生活质量；血压达标时间、血压变异性、低于130 mmHg的时间比例。","8":"安全性监测：设立独立数据安全监察委员会DSMB，预设中期安全分析；连续记录低血压事件SBP<120或需要升压、神经功能恶化、再闭塞、脑水肿/恶性梗死、症状性颅内出血、心肌梗死、心律失常、急性肾损伤和死亡。若干预组出现显著更高的死亡、sICH或严重神经恶化，按O’Brien-Fleming边界考虑暂停或终止试验。"},"ImplementationOrder":["1-2","3-4","5","6-8"],"Dataset":"计划纳入约1200例患者，来自30–50家具有24/7机械取栓能力的卒中中心；收集基线人口学、血管危险因素、卒中严重程度NIHSS、影像ASPECTS/核心体积、闭塞部位、再通等级、围手术期血压连续数据、用药、随访影像和90天mRS。","EvaluationMetrics":{"primary_metric":"90天mRS有序转移的共同优势比，盲法评估并按预设协变量调整。","key_secondary_metric":"90天mRS 0–2比例、24–36小时症状性颅内出血率、早期神经功能恶化率、梗死体积增长、90天死亡率。","safety_metric":"SBP<120或<130持续时间、需要升压治疗、sICH、脑水肿、再闭塞、心肾不良事件和全因死亡。"},"ExpectedOutcome":"若温和目标组提高90天mRS分布且不增加低灌注相关不良事件，将支持机械取栓成功再通后24小时采用SBP 140–160 mmHg并设置明确下限的早期血压管理策略；若无效或有害，则强化继续采用较宽松指南目标并强调避免低血压。"}}

topic: [v15/en/idea_plan/clinical_medicine] Draft a free-text research proposal for a randomized controlled trial comparing telehealth-based cognitive behavioral therapy with usual care for patients with
full_prompt: Draft a free-text research proposal for a randomized controlled trial comparing telehealth-based cognitive behavioral therapy with usual care for patients with chronic insomnia and comorbid depression.
completion:
I’ll ground the trial proposal in current evidence on CBT-I, telehealth delivery, and depression comorbidity before proposing the RCT design.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"telehealth cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia chronic insomnia comorbid depression randomized controlled trial usual care outcomes ISI PHQ-9"}
```

[result]: Simulated search results suggest that CBT-I is guideline-recommended first-line therapy for chronic insomnia and can improve both insomnia severity and depressive symptoms. Digital or telehealth CBT-I has shown feasibility and efficacy, but more pragmatic randomized trials are needed in patients with clinically significant comorbid depression, especially comparing synchronous telehealth CBT-I against usual care.

```action
{"tool":"propose","idea_plan":{"Idea":"Conduct a pragmatic, parallel-group randomized controlled trial evaluating whether telehealth-based cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia improves sleep and depression outcomes compared with usual care among adults with chronic insomnia and comorbid depressive symptoms. The central hypothesis is that structured telehealth CBT-I will produce greater reductions in insomnia severity at 8 weeks and sustained improvements in depressive symptoms, sleep quality, fatigue, and functioning at 3 and 6 months compared with usual care.","ImplementationSteps":{"1":"Study design: A two-arm, assessor-blinded randomized controlled trial with 1:1 allocation to telehealth CBT-I or usual care. The study will enroll adults aged 18 years or older meeting DSM-5 criteria for chronic insomnia disorder and screening positive for at least moderate depressive symptoms, for example PHQ-9 score ≥10. Participants will be recruited from primary care, behavioral health, and sleep medicine clinics.","2":"Eligibility criteria: Inclusion criteria will include insomnia symptoms at least 3 nights per week for at least 3 months, Insomnia Severity Index score ≥15, access to a phone or video-capable device, and willingness to participate in telehealth visits. Exclusion criteria will include untreated obstructive sleep apnea or restless legs syndrome requiring primary treatment, bipolar disorder, psychosis, active substance use disorder requiring acute treatment, severe suicidal ideation requiring urgent intervention, night-shift work, pregnancy if sleep changes are the primary concern, or recent initiation/change of antidepressant or hypnotic medication within the past 4 weeks.","3":"Randomization and masking: Participants will be randomized using a computer-generated sequence stratified by baseline depression severity and current antidepressant use. Outcome assessors and data analysts will be blinded to treatment assignment. Participants and therapists cannot be blinded due to the behavioral nature of the intervention.","4":"Intervention: The experimental arm will receive manualized telehealth CBT-I delivered by trained therapists over 6 weekly sessions by secure video or telephone. Core components will include sleep education, sleep restriction therapy, stimulus control, cognitive restructuring, relaxation strategies, relapse prevention, and coordination with existing depression care when clinically indicated. Participants will complete sleep diaries electronically or by paper.","5":"Comparator: The usual care arm will continue treatment as directed by their clinicians, which may include antidepressant medication, sleep hygiene advice, pharmacotherapy for insomnia, or referral to behavioral health services. To preserve pragmatic relevance, usual care will not be restricted, but all sleep- and mood-related treatments will be tracked.","6":"Outcomes: The primary outcome will be change in Insomnia Severity Index score from baseline to 8 weeks. Key secondary outcomes will include depression severity measured by PHQ-9, sleep quality measured by Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index, fatigue, daytime functioning, anxiety symptoms, hypnotic medication use, treatment satisfaction, and remission rates for insomnia and depression. Outcomes will be assessed at baseline, post-treatment at 8 weeks, and follow-up at 3 and 6 months.","7":"Safety and risk management: Participants endorsing suicidal ideation on PHQ-9 item 9 or during study contacts will undergo protocolized risk assessment, clinician notification when appropriate, and referral to emergency or crisis services if needed. Adverse events, including worsening mood, excessive daytime sleepiness during sleep restriction, falls, or medication changes, will be monitored throughout the trial.","8":"Sample size and analysis: Assuming a clinically meaningful between-group difference of approximately 4 points on the Insomnia Severity Index, the trial will enroll an adequately powered sample allowing for attrition, for example approximately 200 participants total. Analyses will follow the intention-to-treat principle using mixed-effects regression models for repeated measures, adjusting for baseline values and stratification factors. Sensitivity analyses will evaluate adherence, treatment modality, medication changes, and missing data assumptions.","9":"Equity and implementation considerations: The trial will assess feasibility across diverse patient groups, including rural patients and those with limited transportation access. Participants without video access may receive telephone-based sessions. topic: [v15/en/idea_plan/clinical_medicine] Write a free-text research proposal to study postoperative outcomes after enhanced recovery pathways versus standard perioperative care in patients undergoing c
full_prompt: Write a free-text research proposal to study postoperative outcomes after enhanced recovery pathways versus standard perioperative care in patients undergoing colorectal cancer surgery.
completion:
I’ll first check the clinical context and common outcomes used in enhanced recovery after surgery (ERAS) studies for colorectal cancer surgery, then translate that into a concise research proposal.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"enhanced recovery after surgery ERAS colorectal cancer surgery postoperative outcomes length of stay complications readmission standard care study outcomes"}
```

[result]: ERAS pathways in colorectal surgery commonly include preoperative counseling, carbohydrate loading, opioid-sparing analgesia, early mobilization, early feeding, avoidance of routine drains/tubes, and standardized discharge criteria. Frequently studied outcomes include postoperative complications, length of stay, 30-day readmission, mortality, ileus, surgical site infection, time to bowel function, patient-reported recovery, and healthcare costs.

```action
{"tool":"propose","idea_plan":{"Idea":"This study will evaluate whether enhanced recovery after surgery (ERAS) pathways improve postoperative outcomes compared with standard perioperative care among patients undergoing elective colorectal cancer surgery. The proposal will focus on clinically meaningful endpoints including postoperative complications, hospital length of stay, readmission, recovery of bowel function, mortality, and patient-reported recovery. The central hypothesis is that ERAS implementation is associated with shorter hospitalization and faster functional recovery without increasing readmissions or postoperative morbidity.","ImplementationSteps":{"1":"Design a prospective multicenter cohort study or pragmatic controlled study enrolling adult patients undergoing elective colorectal cancer resection. Patients managed under a standardized ERAS pathway will be compared with patients receiving conventional perioperative care, either concurrently at non-ERAS centers or historically before ERAS implementation.","2":"Define eligibility criteria, including adults with confirmed or suspected colorectal cancer undergoing elective colectomy, anterior resection, abdominoperineal resection, or related oncologic colorectal procedures. Exclude emergency surgery, palliative diversion alone, multivisceral procedures with nonstandard recovery pathways, and patients unable to provide follow-up data.","3":"Specify the ERAS intervention components: preoperative education, reduced fasting and carbohydrate loading where appropriate, multimodal opioid-sparing analgesia, goal-directed fluid therapy, prevention of nausea and ileus, early oral intake, early mobilization, and standardized discharge criteria. Standard care will be defined according to usual institutional perioperative practices without a formal ERAS protocol.","4":"Collect baseline variables including age, sex, BMI, comorbidities, ASA class, tumor site and stage, neoadjuvant therapy, surgical approach, stoma formation, operative duration, blood loss, and perioperative complications. These variables will be used to adjust for confounding.","5":"Measure primary outcome as overall postoperative complication rate within 30 days, classified using Clavien-Dindo grading. Secondary outcomes will include length of hospital stay, time to first flatus or bowel movement, time to oral diet tolerance, postoperative ileus, surgical site infection, anastomotic leak, venous thromboembolism, opioid consumption, 30-day readmission, 30-day mortality, patient-reported recovery, and total inpatient cost.","6":"Analyze outcomes using multivariable regression and propensity score adjustment or matching to reduce confounding between ERAS and standard-care groups. Subgroup analyses may examine colon versus rectal cancer surgery, open versus minimally invasive surgery, elderly patients, and patients receiving neoadjuvant therapy.","7":"Assess ERAS protocol adherence by calculating the proportion of completed ERAS elements per patient and evaluating dose-response relationships between adherence level and postoperative outcomes.","8":"Ensure ethical approval, informed consent where required, secure data collection, standardized outcome definitions, and complete 30-day follow-up through clinic visits, telephone review, and electronic health record review."},"ImplementationOrder":["1-2","3","4-5","6","7","8"],"Dataset":"A prospectively collected clinical dataset of adult patients undergoing elective colorectal cancer surgery at participating hospitals. The dataset will include perioperative care pathway exposure, demographic and clinical covariates, operative details, ERAS adherence data, postoperative complications, length of stay, readmissions, mortality, patient-reported recovery measures, and cost data when available.","EvaluationMetrics":{"Primary outcome":"30-day overall postoperative complication rate, graded by Clavien-Dindo classification.","Length of stay":"Number of postoperative hospital days from surgery to discharge, with sensitivity analysis excluding patients with major complications.","Readmission":"Unplanned hospital readmission within 30 days after discharge.","Recovery of bowel function":"Time to first flatus, bowel movement, and tolerance of oral diet.","Major morbidity":"Rate of Clavien-Dindo grade III or higher complications, including anastomotic leak requiring intervention, reoperation, ICU admission, or organ failure.","Mortality":"All-cause 30-day postoperative mortality.","Patient-reported recovery":"Validated recovery or quality-of-life scores collected postoperatively, such as recovery quality, pain, fatigue, mobility, and return to usual activity.","Cost outcome":"Total index hospitalization cost and, if available, 30-day episode-of-care cost."},"ExpectedOutcome":"The study is expected to show that ERAS pathways reduce hospital length of stay and accelerate functional recovery after colorectal cancer surgery without increasing 30-day readmission, mortality, or major postoperative complications. The findings may support broader ERAS implementation, identify which pathway components are most strongly associated with improved outcomes, and provide evidence for standardized perioperative care in colorectal oncology surgery."}}
```

topic: [v15/en/idea_plan/clinical_medicine] Evaluate the clinical utility of an AI-assisted risk prediction tool for detecting early deterioration in hospitalized patients on general medical wards.
full_prompt: Evaluate the clinical utility of an AI-assisted risk prediction tool for detecting early deterioration in hospitalized patients on general medical wards.
completion:
To evaluate clinical utility, I’ll first look for evidence on AI-based early warning systems in general wards, focusing on outcomes beyond discrimination such as escalation, ICU transfer, mortality, and workflow impact.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"AI-assisted early warning score hospitalized general ward clinical utility deterioration randomized pragmatic trial"}
```

[result]: Found relevant evidence themes: machine-learning early warning systems often improve risk discrimination compared with traditional scores, but clinical utility depends on implementation, alert burden, integration with rapid response workflows, clinician trust, and whether alerts lead to timely actionable interventions. Pragmatic evaluations commonly assess ICU transfer, rapid response activation, mortality, length of stay, false alerts, and clinician adoption.

A key next step is to ground the proposal in study designs used for clinical utility evaluation, especially prospective pragmatic trials and implementation studies.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"pragmatic evaluation machine learning early warning system hospitalized patients general wards clinical outcomes"}
```

[result]: Prospective clinical utility studies typically compare AI-assisted deterioration alerts plus usual care versus usual care alone. Strong designs include cluster-randomized, stepped-wedge, or interrupted time-series approaches. Recommended endpoints include unplanned ICU transfer, cardiac arrest, ward mortality, time to escalation, rapid response team activation, alert positive predictive value, alarm burden, and equity across demographic subgroups.

```action

wards, compared with usual monitoring and conventional early warning scores.","ImplementationSteps":{"1":"Conduct a prospective stepped-wedge cluster randomized trial across general medical ward activations, ICU transfers, cardiac arrests, mortality, and length of stay.","EvaluationMetrics":{"Primary clinical outcome":"Composite of unplanned ICU transfer, in-hospital cardiac arrest, or ward [result]: Recent literature highlights that rhythmic auditory stimulation can improve gait parameters after stroke, music-supported therapy may enhance upper-limb motor recovery, and singing-based interventions can support aphasia rehabilitation. Reviews emphasize mechanisms including auditory-motor coupling, reward-mediated neuroplasticity, entrainment, and increased patient engagement.

Rationale: The most promising gap appears to be adaptive personalization: many interventions use music or rhythm, but fewer dynamically tailor musical structure to a patient’s neural, motor, and emotional state.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"adaptive music-supported therapy stroke rehabilitation neuroplasticity rhythmic auditory stimulation"}
```

[result]: Key findings suggest that tempo, rhythmic complexity, musical familiarity, and emotional salience influence motor synchronization and motivation. Wearables, EEG, motion capture, and digital music systems are increasingly feasible in rehabilitation settings, but integrated closed-loop systems remain underdeveloped.

full_prompt: Examine the relationship between microplastic pollution, marine food webs, and coastal community economies. Propose a study design that connects environmental chemistry, ecology, and social science.
completion:
Microplastics link chemical exposure pathways, ecological trophic transfer, and human livelihoods, so I’ll first identify interdisciplinary evidence on those connections, then propose an integrated study design.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"microplastics marine food webs coastal economies interdisciplinary environmental chemistry ecology social science study design"}
```

[result]: Studies show microplastics are found in seawater, sediments, plankton, shellfish, fish, and seabirds; particles can carry additives and sorbed pollutants. Ecological work links ingestion and trophic transfer to altered feeding, growth, and reproduction, while social science research highlights risks to fisheries, aquaculture, tourism, seafood safety perceptions, and coastal livelihoods. Few studies fully integrate chemical characterization, food-web modeling, and socioeconomic impacts in the same coastal system.

```action
{"tool":"read","source":"review on microplastics in marine food webs and socioeconomic impacts"}
```

[result]: Key methodological recommendations include: sampling across environmental compartments and trophic levels; polymer/additive identification using FTIR/Raman/GC-MS; stable isotope or gut-content analysis for food-web position; seafood consumption surveys; valuation of fisheries and tourism impacts; and stakeholder co-design with fishers, aquaculture operators, public health officials, and tourism businesses.

```action

Co-design sampling priorities with fishers, aquaculture operators, tourism businesses, local government, and community representatives.","2":"Collect environmental chemistry samples from surface water, sediments, wastewater outfalls, plankton, bivalves, forage fish, commercial fish, and seabirds or other top consumers where feasible. Quantify microplastic abundance, particle size, morphology, polymer type, and chemical additives or sorbed contaminants using microscopy, FTIR/Raman spectroscopy, and targeted chemical analysis.","3":"Map ecological transfer through the food web using gut-content analysis, stable isotope analysis, and trophic-position modeling. Estimate whether microplastic burdens increase with trophic level, differ by habitat, or correlate with organism health indicators such as body condition, growth, reproductive status, or inflammation biomarkers.","4":"Conduct household, fisher, seafood-market, aquaculture, and tourism surveys to measure seafood consumption, risk perception, trust in seafood safety, livelihood dependence, willingness to pay for certified low-contamination seafood, and reported changes in fishing, marketing, or tourism behavior.","5":"Integrate data in a coupled model linking pollution sources, microplastic concentrations, trophic transfer, seafood exposure pathways, and economic outcomes such as fishery revenue, aquaculture losses, tourism demand, and household vulnerability.","6":"Return results to communities through participatory workshops and evaluate policy scenarios, including improved waste management, stormwater filtration, wastewater upgrades, fishing-gear recovery, seafood monitoring, and public risk communication."},"ImplementationOrder":["1-2","3","4","5","6"],"Dataset":"Newly collected paired dataset from selected coastal sites including microplastic chemistry in water, sediments, and organisms; food-web trophic data; organism health indicators; seafood market data; fisheries and aquaculture revenue records; tourism indicators; and survey/interview data from coastal residents and stakeholders.","EvaluationMetrics":{"microplastic_concentration":"Particles per cubic meter of water, particles per kilogram of sediment, and particles per gram or individual organism tissue.","chemical_profile":"Polymer composition, additive concentrations, and sorbed contaminant loads by site and trophic group.","trophic_transfer":"Relationship between microplastic burden and trophic position estimated from stable isotopes and food-web models.","ecological_health":"Associations between microplastic exposure and body condition, growth, reproductive status, or biomarker responses.","seafood_exposure_risk":"Estimated human exposure from locally consumed seafood based on contamination levels and consumption frequency.","economic_impact":"Estimated changes in fisheries revenue, aquaculture market value, tourism demand, and household livelihood vulnerability under observed and modeled contamination scenarios.","social_response":"Levels of perceived risk, seafood avoidance, trust in institutions, and support for mitigation policies."},"ExpectedOutcome":"The study should identify which pollution sources and habitats contribute most to microplastic exposure, whether particles and associated chemicals move through commercially important food webs, and how ecological risk and public perception translate into economic consequences for coastal communities. I’ll read one representative synthesis to identify practical classroom and ethical design issues.

topic: [v15/en/idea_plan/interdisciplinary] How can Indigenous ecological knowledge and satellite-based remote sensing be combined to improve wildfire risk prediction and land stewardship practices?
full_prompt: How can Indigenous ecological knowledge and satellite-based remote sensing be combined to improve wildfire risk prediction and land stewardship practices?
completion:
To design a strong interdisciplinary plan, I’ll first look for work connecting Indigenous fire stewardship, cultural burning, and remote-sensing wildfire prediction. This helps identify where current models underuse community-held ecological indicators.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"Indigenous ecological knowledge satellite remote sensing wildfire risk prediction cultural burning land stewardship"}
```

[result]: Recent literature highlights that Indigenous fire stewardship can improve fuel management, biodiversity, and landscape resilience, while satellite products such as Landsat, Sentinel-2, MODIS, VIIRS, GEDI, and SMAP are widely used to map vegetation condition, fuel dryness, burn severity, canopy structure, soil moisture, and active fires. Key gap: most wildfire risk models rely on biophysical variables but rarely integrate Indigenous ecological indicators such as culturally significant species condition, seasonal burning calendars, traditional fuel classifications, wind/topography knowledge, and place-based fire histories.

I’ll read a representative source on combining community knowledge with geospatial fire management to shape an ethical and practical implementation pathway.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"community-based participatory mapping Indigenous fire knowledge remote sensing wildfire management"}
```

[result]: Best practices emphasize Indigenous data sovereignty, co-designed research questions, participatory GIS, local control over sensitive cultural data, and model outputs that support community decision-making rather than extract knowledge. Successful approaches combine satellite-derived fuel and moisture indices with Indigenous-led field validation, cultural burn planning, and stewardship priorities.

```action
{"tool":"propose","idea_plan":{"Idea":"Develop a co-produced wildfire risk and stewardship decision-support system that integrates Indigenous ecological knowledge with satellite-based remote sensing. The system would combine satellite indicators of vegetation stress, fuel load, soil moisture, canopy structure, and burn history with Indigenous place-based observations such as traditional fuel categories, seasonal fire calendars, culturally important species health, wind and slope knowledge, and oral histories of past fire behavior. The goal is not only to predict wildfire risk more accurately, but also to support Indigenous-led land stewardship, cultural burning, and ecosystem resilience planning.","ImplementationSteps":{"1":"Establish governance and consent protocols with Indigenous nations or communities, including data sovereignty agreements, benefit-sharing terms, restrictions on sensitive cultural information, and Indigenous leadership in defining research questions and acceptable uses.","2":"Co-design a wildfire risk framework that identifies both remote-sensing variables and Indigenous ecological indicators relevant to local fire behavior, stewardship priorities, cultural burning windows, and ecological health.","3":"Compile satellite datasets such as Landsat and Sentinel-2 vegetation indices, MODIS/VIIRS active fire and thermal anomaly data, SMAP soil moisture, GEDI canopy structure, digital elevation models, weather data, and historical burn severity layers.","4":"Collect Indigenous ecological knowledge through community-approved methods such as participatory mapping, seasonal calendars, field walks, interviews, community workshops, and Indigenous-led monitoring of culturally significant plants, animal habitats, fuel conditions, and fire-sensitive areas.","5":"Translate agreed-upon knowledge into model features only where appropriate, for example local fuel classifications, culturally defined landscape units, seasonal risk thresholds, known wind corridors, or areas where cultural burning historically reduced fuel accumulation.","6":"Train hybrid wildfire risk models using machine learning or Bayesian approaches that can combine quantitative satellite variables with qualitative or categorical community-derived indicators while preserving uncertainty and provenance.","7":"Validate predictions through both conventional accuracy testing against historical fire occurrence and community-based ground truthing, including whether predictions align with local observations of fuel readiness, safe burn windows, and stewardship needs.","8":"Create Indigenous-controlled decision-support outputs such as risk maps, burn-planning dashboards, seasonal alerts, and stewardship priority layers, with access levels determined by the community.","9":"Use the system to support practical land stewardship actions, weather and drought indices, historical fire perimeters and burn severity—with Indigenous community-approved ecological knowledge layers such as local fuel types, cultural burn histories, seasonal fire calendars, culturally important species observations, traditional landscape units, and stewardship priority areas.","EvaluationMetrics":{"PredictionAccuracy":"AUC-ROC, precision-recall, F1 score, and calibration error for wildfire occurrence or high-risk area prediction compared with historical fire records.","SpatialAgreement":"Degree of overlap between predicted high-risk areas and Indigenous-identified areas of concern, measured through participatory validation and spatial agreement statistics.","StewardshipUsefulness":"Community-defined score assessing whether outputs support cultural burning, fuel management, habitat protection, and seasonal planning.","BurnSeverityReduction":"Change in remotely sensed burn severity, such as dNBR or RdNBR, in areas managed using the system compared with unmanaged or conventionally managed areas.","FalseAlarmAndMissRate":"Rates of overprediction and underprediction, especially near communities, cultural sites, and high-value ecological areas.","GovernanceCompliance":"Assessment of whether data access, storage, mapping, and publication follow Indigenous data sovereignty agreements.","EcologicalOutcome":"Trends in vegetation recovery, culturally significant species health, invasive species extent, and habitat condition after stewardship interventions."},"ExpectedOutcome":"The combined approach should produce more locally accurate and culturally relevant wildfire risk predictions than satellite-only models, because Indigenous ecological knowledge can capture fine-scale fuel conditions, seasonal cues, historical fire behavior, and stewardship priorities that satellites may miss. It should also shift wildfire management from reactive suppression toward Indigenous-led prevention, cultural burning, ecosystem restoration, and long-term land resilience while protecting sensitive knowledge through community governance."}}

topic: [v15/en/idea_plan/interdisciplinary] Design an interdisciplinary study on how misinformation spreads during public health crises, incorporating epidemiology, psychology, network science, and media
full_prompt: Design an interdisciplinary study on how misinformation spreads during public health crises, incorporating epidemiology, psychology, network science, and media studies.
completion:
To ground the study design, I’ll first scan for recent interdisciplinary work linking misinformation, public health crises, behavior, and network diffusion.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"public health misinformation spread epidemiology psychology network science media studies interdisciplinary study design"}
```

[result]: Recent literature highlights four converging themes: misinformation can alter protective health behaviors; psychological factors such as trust, threat perception, identity, and cognitive reflection affect susceptibility; network structure influences cascade size and speed; and media ecosystems shape framing, amplification, and cross-platform migration.

I’ll read a representative synthesis to extract design principles for a feasible study.

```action
{"tool":"read","source":"review on health misinformation, social media diffusion, and behavioral response during epidemics"}
```

[result]: Key recommendations include combining social media trace data with survey panels, modeling misinformation as an “information contagion,” linking exposure to behavioral outcomes, distinguishing platform affordances, and evaluating both diffusion metrics and public-health-relevant behavioral consequences.

```action
{"tool":"propose","idea_plan":{"Idea":"Design a mixed-methods interdisciplinary study examining how misinformation spreads during public health crises and how exposure affects public-health behaviors. The study treats misinformation as both a network diffusion process and a behavioral risk factor, integrating epidemiology, psychology, network science, and media studies.","ImplementationSteps":{"1":"Define crisis contexts and misinformation categories: select one or more public health crises, such as COVID-19, mpox, measles outbreaks, or vaccine scares. Build a taxonomy of misinformation themes, including false treatments, vaccine myths, conspiracy narratives, distorted risk claims, and mistrust of institutions.","2":"Collect multi-source data: gather social media posts, news articles, fact-checking records, platform engagement data, and public health timelines. Pair this with survey-panel data measuring misinformation exposure, trust in institutions, perceived risk, political or social identity, media literacy, anxiety, and reported health behaviors.","3":"Epidemiological component: model misinformation exposure as a potential modifier of health behavior, such as vaccination uptake, masking, testing, social distancing, or treatment-seeking. Compare geographic or demographic variation in misinformation prevalence with public health indicators where ethically and statistically appropriate.","4":"Psychology component: test how individual-level factors shape belief and sharing. Measure perceived credibility, emotional arousal, uncertainty, identity threat, trust, and cognitive reflection. Use survey experiments exposing participants to corrected and uncorrected claims to estimate effects on belief, sharing intention, and behavioral intention.","5":"Network science component: reconstruct diffusion networks across platforms where possible. Identify influential nodes, community clusters, cascade structures, echo chambers, bridge accounts, and temporal patterns. Compare misinformation cascades with accurate public health information cascades in speed, reach, persistence, and cross-community spread.","6":"Media studies component: analyze framing, source authority, platform affordances, and narrative style. Examine how misinformation moves between fringe media, mainstream media, influencers, messaging platforms, and official public health communication. Conduct qualitative content analysis of dominant narratives and counternarratives.","7":"Integrative modeling: combine diffusion models, behavioral survey data, and epidemiological indicators. Estimate whether misinformation spread predicts changes in protective behavior after accounting for crisis severity, policy changes, demographics, and baseline media use.","8":"Intervention simulation and testing: simulate countermeasures such as prebunking, rapid correction, trusted messenger campaigns, platform labeling, and community-based public health messaging. Test selected interventions experimentally or quasi-experimentally using survey experiments and natural platform events."},"ImplementationOrder":["1-2","3-6","7-8"],"Dataset":"A combined dataset including social media posts from platforms such as X/Twitter, Reddit, TikTok metadata where available, YouTube transcripts, public Facebook pages, fact-checking databases, news archives, public sample.","EvaluationMetrics":{"diffusion_speed":"Time from first appearance of a misinformation claim to peak sharing or reposting.","cascade_size":"Number of users, posts, reposts, shares, or impressions associated with a misinformation narrative.","network_centrality":"Identification of influential spreaders using degree, betweenness, eigenvector centrality, or PageRank.","cross_platform_migration":"Frequency and timing of misinformation narratives moving across media ecosystems.","belief_accuracy":"Survey-based measure of whether participants correctly identify true and false public health claims.","behavioral_intention":"Self-reported likelihood of vaccination, testing, masking, or seeking medical care.","actual_behavior_proxy":"Observed or reported uptake of public health behaviors, such as vaccination records at aggregate level or self-reported longitudinal behavior.","trust_change":"Change in trust toward public health institutions, clinicians, scientists, media, and community leaders.","correction_effectiveness":"Reduction in belief, sharing intention, or behavioral harm after exposure to corrections, prebunking, or trusted-messenger interventions.","public_health_association":"Statistical association between misinformation prevalence and epidemiological outcomes, controlling for confounders."},"ExpectedOutcome":"The study should produce an integrated explanation of misinformation spread during public health crises: which narratives spread fastest, which network structures amplify them, which psychological factors increase belief and sharing, how media systems shape visibility, and whether misinformation exposure is associated with reduced protective behavior.
